- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""I-deepfix-001 Wave-2a (#1344) — the DETERMINISTIC, FAIL-CLOSED numeric comparator.
2	
3	A cross-source analytical unit may want to co-locate two verified numbers so a reader can compare them
4	("study A reported X%; for comparison, study B reported Y%"). That is only faithful when the two numbers
5	measure the SAME thing: same entity, same measure, same unit, same denominator / baseline / time-window —
6	every discriminating field equal AND positively known. If ANY field is missing or ambiguous, comparing
7	the two numbers is misleading, so this comparator FAILS CLOSED to the neutral connective (under-relax is
8	safe; over-relax is lethal — a wrong "for comparison" across incompatible denominators is a clinical
9	faithfulness violation).
10	
11	THE KEY REUSE (why this is safe by construction): every atomic numeric claim already carries a
12	``normalized_key`` — the conservative merge key built by ``claim_graph._normalized_key_numeric`` (legacy
13	path) or ``claim_graph.build_merge_key`` (redesign path). The REDESIGN key is fully fail-closed: any
14	unknown / defaulted / ambiguous discriminator forces a per-claim ``__unresolved__`` singleton, so a
15	non-singleton redesign key carries every discriminator positively known. The LEGACY key does NOT
16	self-fail-close on every field: ``claim_graph._normalized_key_numeric`` sentinels ONLY on a blank SUBJECT,
17	so a blank predicate / unit / dose / arm / endpoint passes straight through as ``""``. Blank is UNKNOWN,
18	not positively known, so THIS module ENFORCES the missing guard (Fable P1, clinical-safety):
19	``_numeric_comparability_key`` additionally requires EVERY discriminator to be non-blank — a strict
20	tightening that is a NO-OP on redesign keys (already all-non-blank) and CLOSES the legacy blank-unit hole
21	(licensing "for comparison" between %-points and mmol/mol whose unit was never established is the lethal
22	over-relax). This comparator does NOT re-extract, re-parse, or relax anything — it reads the retained key
23	and asks a pure question: are these two numeric claims IDENTICAL on every POSITIVELY-KNOWN discriminator
24	EXCEPT the value, with DIFFERING values? If yes, ``comparison`` is licensed; otherwise ``None``
25	(fail-closed to neutral).
26	
27	VALUE-POSITION INVARIANT (asserted defensively): across ALL three numeric key builders the tag is at
28	index 0 == ``"numeric"`` and the EXACT float value is at index 3:
29	  * legacy  ``_normalized_key_numeric`` -> ``("numeric", subject, predicate, value, unit, dose, arm, endpoint_phrase)``
30	  * redesign ``MERGE_KEY_SPEC[("numeric", "clinical")]``    -> ``[kind_tag, subject, predicate, value, unit, dose, ...]``
31	  * redesign ``MERGE_KEY_SPEC[("numeric", "nonclinical")]`` -> ``[kind_tag, subject, predicate, value, unit, endpoint_phrase]``
32	The comparability key is the merge key with the value slot removed (all discriminators, in order); two
33	claims are comparable iff those discriminator tuples are EQUAL (so their lengths — hence their key builder
34	+ domain — also match). A qualitative / sentinel / short / non-float key => ``None`` (fail-closed).
35	
36	PURE: no network, no model, no faithfulness-file import, no input mutation. Arithmetic is ``==`` / ``!=``
37	over the ALREADY-VERIFIED float values; each clause keeps its OWN provenance token. snake_case. LAW VI:
38	gated by ``PG_NUMERIC_COMPARATOR`` (default OFF) at the call site — when off (or no key lookup threaded)
39	this module is never consulted and the composer is byte-identical.
40	"""
41	from __future__ import annotations
42	
43	import logging
44	import os
45	from typing import Any, Optional
46	
47	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
48	
49	# The comparative RELATION key + its (non-directional) connective phrase live in
50	# ``cross_source_synthesis.LICENSED_CONNECTIVES``; this constant is the relation key the composer sets.
51	NUMERIC_COMPARISON_RELATION = "comparison"
52	
53	# The numeric merge-key tag (index 0) + the EXACT value slot index (index 3) — the cross-builder
54	# invariant documented above. A key that does not match this shape is NOT a comparable numeric claim.
55	_NUMERIC_TAG = "numeric"
56	_VALUE_SLOT_INDEX = 3
57	_MIN_NUMERIC_KEY_LEN = 4  # tag, subject, predicate, value (nonclinical redesign is the shortest at 6)
58	
59	# Wave-3a (I-deepfix-001 #1344, clinical-safety): the LEGACY ``_normalized_key_numeric`` key is a fixed
60	# 8-tuple ``("numeric", subject, predicate, value, unit, dose, arm, endpoint_phrase)`` and it carries a
61	# NO-CUE arm as the non-blank DEFAULT ``"treatment"`` (contradiction_detector.py:1601, kept for OFF
62	# byte-identity — Codex Slice-B P1). ``"treatment"`` is non-blank, so the blank guard in
63	# ``_numeric_comparability_key`` does NOT catch it: two findings whose arm was NEVER positively extracted
64	# (both defaulted to ``"treatment"``) would license a SAME-arm ``comparison`` that was never established —
65	# the lethal over-relax. Mirror ``claim_graph._unknown_arm`` (which treats ``"treatment"`` AS UNKNOWN on
66	# the redesign path): a legacy arm slot == ``"treatment"`` is UNKNOWN -> fail closed. The REDESIGN key
67	# already singleton-forces a defaulted arm UPSTREAM (build_merge_key via _unknown_arm), so a redesign key
68	# NEVER reaches the comparator carrying ``"treatment"`` -> this is a strict NO-OP on redesign keys and
69	# closes the LEGACY path only. arm is the ONLY legacy discriminator with a non-blank unknown default
70	# (dose / endpoint / etc. default to ``""`` — already caught by the blank guard; a defaulted "unknown"
71	# subject is already sentinelled by ``_normalized_key_numeric`` itself).
72	_LEGACY_NUMERIC_KEY_LEN = 8       # ("numeric", subject, predicate, value, unit, dose, arm, endpoint_phrase)
73	_LEGACY_ARM_SLOT_INDEX = 6        # arm position in the full legacy 8-tuple
74	_LEGACY_ARM_UNKNOWN_SENTINEL = "treatment"  # extractor no-cue arm default == UNKNOWN (claim_graph._unknown_arm)
75	
76	_ENV_NUMERIC_COMPARATOR = "PG_NUMERIC_COMPARATOR"
77	
78	# I-deepfix-001 (#1369) STEP 3 — construct-level cross-source numeric comparison. The exact-discriminator
79	# path below only licenses a comparison when two numbers share the SAME subject/entity/unit; that is why
80	# Frey-Osborne "702 occupations / computerisation", Eloundou "1.8% -> 46% of jobs", and ILO "24% of
81	# clerical tasks" — all EXPOSURE_SHARE measures in % — never paired (different subjects) and 892 extracted
82	# numbers rendered zero comparison. The CLOSED, deterministic construct-tag map below lets two numeric
83	# atoms compare when they share a UNIT and a KNOWN construct even with different subject strings. It is
84	# FAIL-CLOSED: an unknown construct (no lexicon hit) is NOT comparable — a construct is NEVER forced.
85	_ENV_CONSTRUCT_COMPARISON = "PG_NUMERIC_CONSTRUCT_COMPARISON"
86	_CONSTRUCT_LEXICON: tuple[tuple[str, tuple[str, ...]], ...] = (
87	    # (construct_tag, needle substrings)  — FIRST match wins (priority order). CLOSED set (Fable/coord spec).
88	    ("EXPOSURE_SHARE", ("exposure", "exposed", "susceptib", "affected", "at-risk", "at risk", "computeris", "computeriz")),
89	    ("LABOR_EFFECT", ("employment", "wage", "displace", "job-loss", "job loss", "jobs lost", "unemploy")),
90	    ("PRODUCTIVITY", ("productivity", "output")),
91	)
92	
93	
94	def numeric_comparator_enabled() -> bool:
95	    """``PG_NUMERIC_COMPARATOR`` gate (default OFF, LAW VI). OFF => the composer never consults the
96	    comparator and the cross-source relation set stays {conflict, agreement, extension, neutral}
97	    (byte-identical). ON => a NEUTRAL pair whose two baskets carry FULLY-comparable numeric claims is
98	    upgraded to the ``comparison`` connective. This is a WEIGHT/CONSOLIDATE surfacing lever, never a
99	    cap / target / thinner (§-1.3)."""
100	    return os.getenv(_ENV_NUMERIC_COMPARATOR, "0").strip().lower() not in ("", "0", "false", "off", "no")
101	
102	
103	def _construct_comparison_enabled() -> bool:
104	    """``PG_NUMERIC_CONSTRUCT_COMPARISON`` gate (default OFF in code, LAW VI; slate pins ON). OFF => only
105	    the exact-discriminator comparison path fires (byte-identical). ON => two positively-known numeric keys
106	    that share a UNIT and a KNOWN construct tag but differ in subject are ALSO licensed for the
107	    non-directional ``comparison`` connective."""
108	    return os.getenv(_ENV_CONSTRUCT_COMPARISON, "0").strip().lower() not in ("", "0", "false", "off", "no")
109	
110	
111	def _construct_tag(normalized_key: Any) -> Optional[tuple]:
112	    """``(construct_tag, unit)`` for a positively-known numeric key, or ``None`` (fail-closed).
113	
114	    ``construct_tag`` is the FIRST-matching CLOSED lexicon entry over the key's subject+predicate text;
115	    no hit => ``None`` (an unknown construct is never forced to compare). ``unit`` is the merge key's unit
116	    discriminator (legacy layout: original index 4 => discriminators[3]); the comparability view has
117	    already fail-closed on a BLANK unit, so ``unit`` here is always positively known. Pure; never raises."""
118	    view = _numeric_comparability_key(normalized_key)
119	    if view is None:
120	        return None
121	    disc, _val = view
122	    # disc = (tag, subject, predicate, unit, dose, arm, endpoint) on the legacy layout; scan the SEMANTIC
123	    # fields (everything past the "numeric" tag) for the construct lexicon.
124	    text = " ".join(str(d) for d in disc[1:]).lower()
125	    tag: Optional[str] = None
126	    for cand, needles in _CONSTRUCT_LEXICON:
127	        if any(n in text for n in needles):
128	            tag = cand
129	            break
130	    if tag is None:
131	        return None
132	    unit = str(disc[3]).strip().lower() if len(disc) > 3 else ""
133	    return (tag, unit)
134	
135	
136	def _numeric_comparability_key(normalized_key: Any) -> Optional[tuple]:
137	    """The comparability view of a numeric ``normalized_key``: ``(discriminators_tuple, value_float)``
138	    where ``discriminators_tuple`` is the merge key with the value slot removed (every discriminator, in
139	    order) and ``value_float`` is the EXACT verified value.
140	
141	    Returns ``None`` (FAIL-CLOSED — treated as not-comparable) for anything that is not a
142	    FULLY-positively-known numeric merge key:
143	      * a non-tuple / too-short key;
144	      * a key whose tag (index 0) is not ``"numeric"`` — i.e. a qualitative key, the legacy
145	        ``__numeric_unknown__`` sentinel, or the redesign ``__unresolved__`` singleton;
146	      * a key whose value slot (index 3) is not a real number;
147	      * a key with ANY BLANK discriminator (empty / whitespace). The REDESIGN key never reaches here with
148	        a blank discriminator (``build_merge_key`` already singleton-forces on any unknown field), but the
149	        LEGACY ``_normalized_key_numeric`` sentinels ONLY on a blank SUBJECT — a blank predicate / unit /
150	        dose / arm / endpoint passes straight through as ``""``. Blank is UNKNOWN, not positively known, so
151	        this module ENFORCES the missing guard HERE (Fable P1, clinical-safety): any blank discriminator
152	        fails closed. This is a strict tightening (NO-OP on redesign keys) — never compare two numbers
153	        whose unit / entity / baseline was never established.
154	    Pure; never raises."""
155	    if not isinstance(normalized_key, tuple) or len(normalized_key) < _MIN_NUMERIC_KEY_LEN:
156	        return None
157	    if normalized_key[0] != _NUMERIC_TAG:
158	        return None
159	    value = normalized_key[_VALUE_SLOT_INDEX]
160	    # bool is an int subclass but is never a real measured value — reject it explicitly.
161	    if isinstance(value, bool) or not isinstance(value, (int, float)):
162	        return None
163	    discriminators = normalized_key[:_VALUE_SLOT_INDEX] + normalized_key[_VALUE_SLOT_INDEX + 1:]
164	    # Fable P1 (clinical-safety): EVERY discriminator field beyond the ``"numeric"`` tag must be POSITIVELY
165	    # KNOWN (non-blank). The legacy key does NOT self-fail-close on a blank non-subject field, and comparing
166	    # two values across an unknown unit / entity / baseline (%-points vs mmol/mol) is the lethal over-relax.
167	    # A single blank/empty/whitespace discriminator => fail closed. (``discriminators[0]`` is the tag.)
168	    for slot in discriminators[1:]:
169	        if not str(slot).strip():
170	            return None
171	    # Wave-3a (clinical-safety, mirrors claim_graph._unknown_arm): the LEGACY key carries a NO-CUE arm as
172	    # the non-blank sentinel ``"treatment"`` (index 6 of the fixed 8-tuple), which the blank guard above
173	    # does NOT catch. Two arm-unknown findings both default to ``"treatment"``; licensing a comparison
174	    # implies a same-arm claim that was NEVER positively established (the lethal over-relax). Fail closed on
175	    # it. LEGACY key ONLY (len == 8): the redesign key already singleton-forces a defaulted arm upstream, so
176	    # it never reaches here carrying ``"treatment"`` -> strict NO-OP on redesign keys.
177	    if (
178	        len(normalized_key) == _LEGACY_NUMERIC_KEY_LEN
179	        and str(normalized_key[_LEGACY_ARM_SLOT_INDEX]).strip().lower() == _LEGACY_ARM_UNKNOWN_SENTINEL
180	    ):
181	        return None
182	    return (discriminators, float(value))
183	
184	
185	def license_numeric_comparison(key_a: Any, key_b: Any) -> Optional[str]:
186	    """Decide whether a COMPARATIVE connective is licensed between two claim clusters, from their retained
187	    numeric ``normalized_key`` tuples.
188	
189	    Returns ``NUMERIC_COMPARISON_RELATION`` (``"comparison"``) IFF:
190	      * both keys reduce to a comparability view (both are positively-known numeric merge keys), AND
191	      * their discriminator tuples are EQUAL — every field (measure/entity/unit/denominator/baseline/
192	        time-window as carried by the merge-key spec) matches AND is positively known, AND
193	      * the two verified values DIFFER (equal values would already have shared a cluster — there is no
194	        "comparison" to draw between two identical numbers).
195	    Otherwise ``None`` (FAIL-CLOSED to the neutral connective): any missing / ambiguous / differing
196	    discriminator, a non-numeric key, or equal values. Pure; deterministic; arithmetic is ``==`` / ``!=``
197	    over already-verified floats. NEVER asserts a direction (larger/smaller) — the connective is
198	    non-directional and each clause keeps its own token."""
199	    ca = _numeric_comparability_key(key_a)
200	    cb = _numeric_comparability_key(key_b)
201	    if ca is None or cb is None:
202	        return None
203	    disc_a, val_a = ca
204	    disc_b, val_b = cb
205	    if val_a == val_b:
206	        return None  # identical values => same claim, no comparison to draw
207	    if disc_a == disc_b:
208	        return NUMERIC_COMPARISON_RELATION  # exact claim-identity comparison (measure/unit/entity match)
209	    # I-deepfix-001 (#1369) STEP 3: construct-level fallback — DIFFERENT subjects but SAME unit + SAME
210	    # KNOWN construct (Frey-Osborne vs Eloundou vs ILO, all EXPOSURE_SHARE in %). ``_construct_tag`` returns
211	    # ``(construct_tag, unit)`` and is None on an unknown construct, so the equality below requires BOTH the
212	    # construct AND the unit to match — a different construct (exposure % vs wage pp) or a different unit
213	    # never pairs (fail-closed). Values already differ (guarded above). The connective stays the
214	    # non-directional "comparison"; each clause keeps its own [#ev] token and no direction/magnitude is
215	    # asserted, so faithfulness is preserved by quotation (§-1.3). Gated by PG_NUMERIC_CONSTRUCT_COMPARISON.
216	    if _construct_comparison_enabled():
217	        cta = _construct_tag(key_a)
218	        ctb = _construct_tag(key_b)
219	        if cta is not None and cta == ctb:
220	            return NUMERIC_COMPARISON_RELATION
221	    return None
222	
223	
224	def build_numeric_key_lookup(claims: Any) -> dict[str, tuple]:
225	    """``{claim_cluster_id: normalized_key}`` for every ``kind == "numeric"`` atomic claim.
226	
227	    All claims in a cluster share the SAME ``normalized_key`` by construction (that is what clustered
228	    them), so a last-writer-wins map is well-defined. A claim with no ``claim_cluster_id`` or no
229	    ``normalized_key`` is skipped. The composer threads this map so a basket's ``claim_cluster_id`` can be
230	    resolved to its numeric merge key for the comparator. Pure; empty on ``None`` / non-numeric input."""
231	    out: dict[str, tuple] = {}
232	    for claim in (claims or []):
233	        try:
234	            if str(getattr(claim, "kind", "") or "").strip().lower() != _NUMERIC_TAG:
235	                continue
236	            ccid = str(getattr(claim, "claim_cluster_id", "") or "")
237	            key = getattr(claim, "normalized_key", None)
238	        except AttributeError:
239	            continue
240	        if not ccid or not isinstance(key, tuple):
241	            continue
242	        out[ccid] = key
243	    return out
244

1	"""S4 OUTLINE — cp4 output-boundary checkpoint (Design 5 §4 + master §5 envelope).
2	
3	The S4 section boundary: ``cp3_basket_snapshot.json`` in -> ``cp4_outline_snapshot.json`` out.
4	This file holds the orchestrator's PRODUCT — the final section plans + the full revision audit
5	trail (every op, reason, round; rejected + deferred ops) + digest stats — as DATA ONLY. A
6	resume re-enters at section composition with these plans and RE-RUNS every faithfulness gate;
7	it can NEVER replay a stored decision (§-1.3 ABSOLUTE). The forbidden-verdict-key guard mirrors
8	``run_honest_sweep_r3.load_a12_checkpoint`` (``:6936-6974``) but recurses into nested dicts so a
9	verdict cannot hide inside the revision audit payload.
10	
11	SEAM (WP-0a): when the shared ``generator/checkpoint_envelope.py`` (master §5) lands, the write/
12	load here delegate to it — this module already mirrors the §5 envelope field-for-field
13	(``schema_version`` / ``stage`` / ``question_sha`` / ``upstream`` hash-chain / ``flag_slate`` /
14	``run_config_sha`` / ``adjustments_applied`` / DATA-only payload) so that swap is a delegation,
15	not a schema change. Atomic write (temp + ``os.replace``), sorted-keys deterministic bytes.
16	Best-effort write (never abort a paid run); fail-loud read (corrupt / verdict-leaked never
17	silently loads — LAW II).
18	"""
19	
20	from __future__ import annotations
21	
22	import json
23	import logging
24	import os
25	from pathlib import Path
26	from typing import Any, Mapping, Sequence
27	
28	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
29	
30	CP4_OUTLINE_SNAPSHOT_FILENAME = "cp4_outline_snapshot.json"
31	CP4_SCHEMA_VERSION = 1
32	CP4_STAGE = "outline"
33	
34	# The verdict tokens a DATA-ONLY checkpoint may NEVER contain, at ANY nesting depth.
35	# Mirrors _A12_FORBIDDEN_VERDICT_KEYS (run_honest_sweep_r3.py:6936) — a resume re-runs every
36	# gate from the reloaded DATA and can never replay a stored decision.
37	_CP4_FORBIDDEN_VERDICT_KEYS = frozenset(
38	    {
39	        "release_outcome",
40	        "release_allowed",
41	        "released",
42	        "verified",
43	        "is_verified",
44	        "final_verdicts",
45	        "d8_decision",
46	        "four_role_evaluation",
47	    }
48	)
49	
50	_FAITHFULNESS_INVARIANT = (
51	    "DATA ONLY; no verdict/release_outcome stored anywhere in this checkpoint; a resume "
52	    "re-enters at section composition with these plans and MUST re-run every faithfulness "
53	    "gate (strict_verify / NLI / 4-role / D8) — it can never replay a stored decision."
54	)
55	
56	
57	def _assert_no_verdict_keys(obj: Any, path: str = "$") -> None:
58	    """Recursively refuse any forbidden verdict key anywhere in the payload (fail loud)."""
59	    if isinstance(obj, Mapping):
60	        leaked = sorted(_CP4_FORBIDDEN_VERDICT_KEYS & set(map(str, obj.keys())))
61	        if leaked:
62	            raise ValueError(
63	                f"cp4 outline checkpoint contains FORBIDDEN verdict key(s) {leaked} at {path} — "
64	                "a checkpoint stores DATA ONLY (§-1.3); a resume re-runs every gate and can NEVER "
65	                "replay a stored decision. Refusing."
66	            )
67	        for key, value in obj.items():
68	            _assert_no_verdict_keys(value, f"{path}.{key}")
69	    elif isinstance(obj, (list, tuple)):
70	        for idx, value in enumerate(obj):
71	            _assert_no_verdict_keys(value, f"{path}[{idx}]")
72	
73	
74	def build_cp4_payload(
75	    *,
76	    question_sha: str,
77	    upstream: Sequence[Mapping[str, str]] | None,
78	    run_config_sha: str,
79	    flag_slate: Mapping[str, str] | None,
80	    adjustments_applied: Sequence[Any] | None,
81	    final_plans: Sequence[Mapping[str, Any]],
82	    revision_audit: Mapping[str, Any] | None,
83	    digest_stats: Mapping[str, Any] | None,
84	) -> dict[str, Any]:
85	    """Assemble the cp4 payload (envelope §5 fields + DATA-only ``payload``). Rejects a verdict
86	    leak eagerly so a bad build fails at write time, not silently on the next resume."""
87	    payload = {
88	        "schema_version": CP4_SCHEMA_VERSION,
89	        "stage": CP4_STAGE,
90	        "question_sha": str(question_sha or ""),
91	        "upstream": [dict(u) for u in (upstream or [])],
92	        "run_config_sha": str(run_config_sha or ""),
93	        "flag_slate": dict(flag_slate or {}),
94	        "adjustments_applied": list(adjustments_applied or []),
95	        "payload": {
96	            "final_plans": [dict(p) for p in final_plans],
97	            "revision_audit": dict(revision_audit or {}),
98	            "digest_stats": dict(digest_stats or {}),
99	        },
100	        "faithfulness_invariant": _FAITHFULNESS_INVARIANT,
101	    }
102	    _assert_no_verdict_keys(payload)
103	    return payload
104	
105	
106	def write_cp4_outline_snapshot(run_dir: str | Path, payload: Mapping[str, Any]) -> Path | None:
107	    """Atomically write the cp4 snapshot (temp + ``os.replace``, sorted-keys). Best-effort:
108	    a write failure must NOT abort a paid run — returns None on failure. ``payload`` is validated
109	    for verdict leaks before any bytes hit disk."""
110	    try:
111	        _assert_no_verdict_keys(payload)
112	        target = Path(run_dir) / CP4_OUTLINE_SNAPSHOT_FILENAME
113	        tmp = target.with_suffix(target.suffix + ".tmp")
114	        tmp.write_text(
115	            json.dumps(payload, indent=2, sort_keys=True, default=str) + "\n",
116	            encoding="utf-8",
117	        )
118	        os.replace(tmp, target)
119	        return target
120	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — checkpoint durability is best-effort, never a run blocker
121	        # item 11: keep best-effort semantics (return None, never abort a paid run) but LOG the failure
122	        # so a run silently losing its cp4 checkpoint leaves a trace instead of vanishing (a verdict
123	        # leak caught here also surfaces via this warning rather than being swallowed without a word).
124	        logger.warning(
125	            "cp4 outline checkpoint write FAILED (best-effort, run continues without a cp4 snapshot "
126	            "at %s): %s", run_dir, exc,
127	        )
128	        return None
129	
130	
131	def load_cp4_outline_snapshot(run_dir: str | Path) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
132	    """Load the cp4 snapshot as DATA ONLY. Returns None if absent. Fail-loud: corrupt JSON, a
133	    non-object payload, OR any forbidden verdict key (at any depth) raises — never a silent load."""
134	    path = Path(run_dir) / CP4_OUTLINE_SNAPSHOT_FILENAME
135	    if not path.is_file():
136	        return None
137	    with path.open(encoding="utf-8") as handle:
138	        data = json.load(handle)  # raises on corrupt JSON -> fail loud
139	    if not isinstance(data, dict):
140	        raise ValueError(f"cp4 outline checkpoint is not a JSON object: {path}")
141	    _assert_no_verdict_keys(data)
142	    return data
143

1	"""S4 OUTLINE — ORCH-1 basket-digest menu + ORCH-2 requirements block (Design 5, ruling R2).
2	
3	FS-Researcher completion piece C2: the outline planner must read CONSOLIDATED-CLAIM
4	DIGESTS (claim text + corroboration + tier mix + member ev_ids) — the semantic
5	equivalent of the paper's ``knowledge_base/`` — NOT bare row titles. Today the large-pool
6	outline menu terses every row to ``ev_id + tier + title`` and drops the statement
7	(``multi_section_generator.py:2636-2666``), so the planner assigns ev_ids to sections while
8	never seeing what the rows SAY. This module builds the richer menu by CONSOLIDATION
9	(baskets), never by dropping rows (§-1.3 weight-and-consolidate): a many-source claim shows
10	as ONE heavy basket line instead of N title rows, and every pool row is accounted for
11	(basket member OR singleton line) — the 100%-of-pool honesty invariant.
12	
13	Pure: no LLM, no network, deterministic (LAW V one-responsibility). The sanitizer is
14	INJECTED (identity in tests) so importing this module pulls no generator-package deps.
15	
16	ORCH-2 requirements block: renders the user's deliverable asks (required sections / order,
17	audience, tone, reference style, length ask) + explicit scope constraints into a text block
18	the caller appends to the outline USER prompt. Empty spec => "" => byte-identical no-append.
19	The spec is read via ``.get`` so it works against either Design 3's DeliverableSpec (once
20	WP-1b lands) or a plain protocol dict today — build-to-interface, no fake stand-in.
21	
22	PUSH A (same-work-aware digest, Fable push-to-ceiling): ``build_outline_digest`` gains an
23	OPTIONAL ``same_work_groups`` argument — the exact cp3 ``payload.same_work_groups`` shape
24	(``{member_evidence_ids, canonical_index, same_work_id}``). When supplied it (a) renders each
25	basket's corroboration at WORK level (dedupe members through the alias map) as ``xK works (N
26	rows)`` while KEEPING every member ev_id disclosed (§-1.3 consolidate, drop nothing), and (b)
27	collapses same-work SINGLETON copies into ONE line carrying the canonical ev_id + its alias
28	ids — every alias still accounted for by ``covered_ev_ids()`` so the 100%-of-pool invariant
29	holds. ``same_work_groups=None`` (the default) is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the pre-PUSH-A behaviour.
30	"""
31	
32	from __future__ import annotations
33	
34	import logging
35	import os
36	import re
37	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
38	from typing import Any, Callable, Mapping, Sequence
39	
40	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
41	
42	# ── knob defaults (LAW VI; exact names/defaults from Design 5 §6 + master §6). These are
43	# the resolver-swap seam per master §1.5: when run_config.py (WP-0b) lands, each read below
44	# becomes ``run_config.get(<id>)`` — same names, same defaults, byte-identical when unset.
45	# PUSH 3 (de-starve the digest, §9.1.8 read-the-API-don't-guess): the prior 60000 default
46	# STARVED a routine 686-row cp3 pool — its FULL verbose digest is 85712 chars, so the guard
47	# tersed all 512 singletons to title-only (degraded=True) even though the tersed menu was
48	# only 54145 chars. The serving model is GLM-5.2 (OpenRouter `z-ai/glm-5.2`): context_length
49	# 1,048,576 tokens, top-provider max_completion 131,072 (queried live, not guessed). The outline
50	# INPUT budget = context - reasoning(PG_OUTLINE_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS=6144) - content
51	# (PG_OUTLINE_MIN_MAX_TOKENS=16384) - system prompt; 200000 chars is ~50K input tokens, which
52	# leaves >20% margin against even a conservative 200K-token GLM-5.x context floor and is trivial
53	# against GLM-5.2's real 1M window. This is >2x the measured full-verbose size (85712), so the
54	# routine cp3 pool renders degraded=False with room to spare. The guard MECHANISM is unchanged —
55	# only the default budget rose (LAW VI: still env-overridable via PG_OUTLINE_DIGEST_MAX_CHARS).
56	PG_OUTLINE_DIGEST_MAX_CHARS_DEFAULT = 200000
57	
58	_CLAIM_MAX_CHARS = 200          # basket representative claim, per Design 5 ORCH-1 (~200c)
59	_TITLE_MAX_CHARS = 120          # singleton title, matches the small-pool menu (:2616)
60	_STATEMENT_MAX_CHARS = 160      # singleton statement, matches the small-pool menu (:2617)
61	# Item 2 (same-work TITLE fallback): the MINIMUM normalized-title length that may fold two rows as
62	# one work. Long enough that a distinctive paper title ("GPTs are GPTs..." -> ~40 alnum chars) folds
63	# while a short/generic heading (two DIFFERENT OECD reg-policy docs) does NOT false-merge (§-1.3: a
64	# fold keeps ALL rows; a false fold would misstate corroboration, so the guard stays conservative).
65	_TITLE_FALLBACK_MIN_CHARS = 25
66	
67	
68	def _env_int(name: str, default: int) -> int:
69	    """Read an int env knob, falling back to ``default`` on unset/garbage (LAW VI)."""
70	    try:
71	        return int(os.getenv(name, default))
72	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
73	        return int(default)
74	
75	
76	def _identity_sanitizer(text: str) -> tuple[str, int]:
77	    """Fallback used only if the real provenance sanitizer is not injected AND not
78	    importable. Never silently strips content — returns the text unchanged."""
79	    return text, 0
80	
81	
82	def _default_sanitizer() -> Callable[[str], tuple[str, int]]:
83	    """Resolve the real provenance sanitizer lazily so importing this module needs no
84	    generator-package dependency. Tests inject an identity sanitizer directly.
85	
86	    Item 15: the fallback is narrowed to ``ImportError`` ONLY and LOGGED loudly. Previously a bare
87	    ``except Exception`` swallowed ANY failure inside the sanitizer (a bug, a bad signature) and
88	    silently returned identity — which DISABLES the §9.1.7 prompt-injection defense without a trace.
89	    Now a genuine sanitizer error PROPAGATES (fail loud), and the only-legitimate offline/unit case
90	    (the module is not importable) still fails open to identity BUT emits a WARNING so the disabled
91	    defense is DISCLOSED, never silent (§-1.3 fail-loud-never-silent)."""
92	    try:
93	        from src.polaris_graph.generator.provenance_generator import (
94	            sanitize_evidence_text,
95	        )
96	
97	        return sanitize_evidence_text
98	    except ImportError as exc:  # offline/unit context ONLY: fail open to identity, but DISCLOSE it
99	        logger.warning(
100	            "outline_digest: real provenance sanitizer unimportable (%s) — falling back to the "
101	            "IDENTITY sanitizer; the §9.1.7 prompt-injection defense is DISABLED for this digest "
102	            "build. Inject a real sanitizer in production.", exc,
103	        )
104	        return _identity_sanitizer
105	
106	
107	@dataclass
108	class OutlineDigestMenu:
109	    """The consolidated-claim menu the outline planner reads instead of bare titles."""
110	
111	    basket_lines: list[str]                 # one line per multi-member finding cluster
112	    singleton_lines: list[str]              # one line per row in no multi-member cluster
113	    ev_id_to_basket: dict[str, str]         # member ev_id -> basket id (full map; never elided)
114	    total_chars: int                        # rendered menu size (headroom accounting)
115	    degraded: bool = False                  # a headroom-guard terse pass fired
116	    # item 11: True when EVEN the fully-tersed+elided menu is STILL over ``max_chars`` — a
117	    # pathological pool that would ship a silently oversized prompt. Disclosed (never silent): the
118	    # builder also logs a WARNING, and the caller must surface/act on this flag (fail loud).
119	    oversized: bool = False
120	    basket_member_ev_ids: dict[str, list[str]] = field(default_factory=dict)
121	    # PUSH A: canonical singleton ev_id -> its FOLDED same-work alias ev_ids (the copies of the
122	    # same underlying work that collapsed onto that one singleton line). Empty when
123	    # ``same_work_groups`` was not supplied. ``covered_ev_ids()`` unions these so every alias is
124	    # still accounted for by the 100%-of-pool invariant (§-1.3 consolidate — folded, never dropped).
125	    singleton_alias_ev_ids: dict[str, list[str]] = field(default_factory=dict)
126	    # PUSH A: basket id -> WORK-level corroboration (distinct works among the basket's members,
127	    # deduped through the same-work alias map). Equals the row count when no members share a work
128	    # (so it is byte-identical to ``len(members)`` when ``same_work_groups`` is absent). This is the
129	    # honest corroboration the orphan check reads — 4 rows of 2 works corroborate a claim TWICE.
130	    basket_work_corroboration: dict[str, int] = field(default_factory=dict)
131	    # Item 4 observability tripwire: how many DOI groups hit the false-merge REFUSAL branch
132	    # (``_build_alias_map``: ``len(stamped) >= 2`` — TWO OR MORE distinct cp3 works share one DOI, so
133	    # the fold declines to merge them and folds only the UNCLAIMED members). Empirically ZERO on the
134	    # real 346-basket cp3 corpus (0 DOIs span >= 2 cp3 groups). Surfaced (never silent) so if that
135	    # zero-incidence assumption ever breaks on a future corpus it is VISIBLE — a non-zero count means
136	    # the digest is declining a DOI merge and a key-level union-find remap may be warranted.
137	    doi_false_merge_guard_hits: int = 0
138	    # Symmetric counter for the TITLE false-merge refusal branch (two cp3 works share one title).
139	    title_false_merge_guard_hits: int = 0
140	
141	    def render(self) -> str:
142	        """The prompt menu text — baskets first (heaviest claims), then singletons."""
143	        return "\n".join(self.basket_lines + self.singleton_lines)
144	
145	    def covered_ev_ids(self) -> set[str]:
146	        """Every ev_id the menu accounts for (basket member OR singleton OR a folded alias)."""
147	        covered = set(self.ev_id_to_basket)
148	        for line in self.singleton_lines:
149	            # singleton lines start with the canonical ev_id token (see _singleton_line)
150	            covered.add(line.split(" ", 1)[0])
151	        # PUSH A: same-work aliases collapsed onto a singleton line are still covered rows —
152	        # union them explicitly (the line only prints the canonical head token).
153	        for canonical, aliases in self.singleton_alias_ev_ids.items():
154	            covered.add(canonical)
155	            covered.update(aliases)
156	        return covered
157	
158	
159	def _ev_id_at(evidence: Sequence[Mapping[str, Any]], index: int) -> str:
160	    """Resolve a cluster member index to its ev_id, fail-loud on a corrupt index
161	    (a clusters/evidence mismatch is a real bug, never silently papered over — LAW II)."""
162	    if not (0 <= index < len(evidence)):
163	        raise ValueError(
164	            f"finding cluster references evidence index {index} outside the pool of "
165	            f"{len(evidence)} rows — clusters and evidence are misaligned (refusing to "
166	            "build a garbage outline menu)."
167	        )
168	    return str(evidence[index].get("evidence_id", "") or "")
169	
170	
171	# Item 3a/10: leading fetch/format chrome tokens that must be peeled off a TITLE before it is
172	# normalized to a work key. "(PDF) GPTs are GPTs" must key IDENTICALLY to "GPTs are GPTs" — the
173	# leading "pdf" is alphanumeric, so it survives the alnum-normalize and would otherwise SPLIT the
174	# same work into two. Exact known tokens only (content-integrity, not a lexical quality guess).
175	_TITLE_CHROME_PREFIXES = ("[pdf]", "(pdf)", "url source:", "pdf source:")
176	
177	
178	def _strip_title_chrome(title: str) -> str:
179	    """Peel leading chrome tokens (``[pdf]`` / ``(pdf)`` / ``url source:``) and trailing ellipsis
180	    (``...`` / unicode ``…``) off a title so a chrome-prefixed or truncated title normalizes to the
181	    SAME key as its clean form (item 3a). Loops so stacked prefixes ("[PDF] URL Source:") all peel.
182	    Never drops the row — only cleans the KEY input (§-1.3: a fold keeps every row, it only reports
183	    them as one work)."""
184	    t = (title or "").strip()
185	    changed = True
186	    while changed:
187	        changed = False
188	        low = t.lower()
189	        for pref in _TITLE_CHROME_PREFIXES:
190	            if low.startswith(pref):
191	                t = t[len(pref):].strip()
192	                changed = True
193	                break
194	    while t.endswith("...") or t.endswith("…"):
195	        t = (t[:-3] if t.endswith("...") else t[:-1]).rstrip()
196	    return t
197	
198	
199	# Item 5 (same-work DOI fold): a byte-for-byte mirror of ``finding_dedup._normalize_doi`` /
200	# ``weighted_enrichment._normalize_doi``. This module deliberately never imports finding_dedup
201	# (LAW V one-responsibility, zero generator-package deps), so the shared DOI contract is copied
202	# here verbatim. Keep in lockstep with finding_dedup:_DOI_PREFIX_RE / _normalize_doi.
203	_DOI_PREFIX_RE = re.compile(r"^(?:doi:|https?://(?:dx\.)?doi\.org/)", re.IGNORECASE)
204	
205	
206	def _normalize_doi(doi: Any) -> str:
207	    """Canonical DOI for same-work grouping (SHARED contract with finding_dedup._normalize_doi).
208	
209	    Lowercase → strip a leading ``doi:`` / ``https://doi.org/`` / ``http://dx.doi.org/`` prefix →
210	    trim → ``rstrip("/")``. A usable DOI starts with the ``10.`` registrant prefix; anything else
211	    is noise. Returns ``""`` for a missing / blank / non-``10.`` DOI (it never groups two works)."""
212	    text = str(doi or "").strip().lower()
213	    if not text:
214	        return ""
215	    text = _DOI_PREFIX_RE.sub("", text).strip().rstrip("/")
216	    return text if text.startswith("10.") else ""
217	
218	
219	def _normalized_title_key(title: str) -> str | None:
220	    """Item 2 (same-work TITLE fallback): a work_key derived from a source TITLE, for folding
221	    TITLE-IDENTICAL works the cp3 URL/DOI ``same_work_groups`` missed (the same paper posted at two
222	    URLs — an arXiv PDF + a GovAI mirror of "GPTs are GPTs", or Noy_Zhang_1.pdf + "Experimental
223	    Evidence on the Productivity Effects..."). Lowercase, keep only alphanumerics. Returns
224	    ``title:<norm>`` ONLY when the normalized title is >= ``_TITLE_FALLBACK_MIN_CHARS`` — a
225	    short/generic title (two DIFFERENT OECD reg-policy docs) returns ``None`` and is NEVER folded,
226	    so the fallback cannot false-merge distinct works. Never a drop (§-1.3): a fold keeps ALL rows,
227	    it only reports them as ONE work for corroboration.
228	
229	    Item 3a: leading fetch/format chrome ("(PDF)", "URL Source:") and trailing ellipsis are peeled
230	    FIRST (``_strip_title_chrome``), so a chrome-prefixed or truncated title keys IDENTICALLY to its
231	    clean form instead of splitting the same underlying work into two."""
232	    norm = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]", "", _strip_title_chrome(title).lower())
233	    if len(norm) < _TITLE_FALLBACK_MIN_CHARS:
234	        return None
235	    return f"title:{norm}"
236	
237	
238	def _build_alias_map(
239	    same_work_groups: Sequence[Mapping[str, Any]] | None,
240	    evidence: Sequence[Mapping[str, Any]] | None = None,
241	    *,
242	    stats: dict[str, int] | None = None,
243	) -> dict[str, str]:
244	    """ev_id -> work_key from the cp3 ``same_work_groups`` payload (PUSH A) + the item-2 TITLE fold.
245	
246	    Each group carries ``member_evidence_ids`` (all corroborating rows of ONE underlying work)
247	    and a stable ``same_work_id`` string (``url:`` / ``doi:`` / ``title:`` key). The work_key is
248	    that ``same_work_id`` (falling back to ``canon:<canonical_index>`` only if the id is blank).
249	    ``setdefault`` keeps the first group to claim an ev_id, so the map is deterministic regardless
250	    of group order. Returns ``{}`` when no groups are supplied AND no ``evidence`` is passed (=>
251	    every row is its own work => byte-identical to the pre-PUSH-A path).
252	
253	    Item 2 (normalized-TITLE fallback): when ``evidence`` is supplied, rows the cp3 groups did NOT
254	    unify are folded by ``_normalized_title_key`` whenever >= 2 rows share ONE distinctive (>=25
255	    alnum-char) title — the same underlying work the URL/DOI ``same_work_id`` missed (measured on
256	    cp4: >=12 title-identical work groups the 51 URL/DOI groups left split, e.g. eloundou "GPTs are
257	    GPTs" + its GovAI mirror). Deterministic (evidence order = canonical). A title group that
258	    OVERLAPS an existing cp3 group ADOPTS that group's key (so the two are UNIFIED into one work,
259	    not left as two); a title group the cp3 groups never touched takes the ``title:`` key itself.
260	    ``evidence=None`` => no title fold (byte-identical to the cp3-only map).
261	
262	    Item 5 (normalized-DOI fold, runs BEFORE the title fold): rows sharing ONE usable ``10.`` DOI
263	    are folded into one work even when their URLs differ (the same-DOI-different-URL copies the cp3
264	    URL ``same_work_id`` left split). Same overlap/adopt + false-merge-guard rules as item 2, keyed
265	    on ``_normalize_doi``. Byte-identical when no >= 2 rows share a DOI."""
266	    alias_of: dict[str, str] = {}
267	    for group in (same_work_groups or []):
268	        if not isinstance(group, Mapping):
269	            continue
270	        work_key = str(group.get("same_work_id") or "").strip()
271	        if not work_key:
272	            canonical_index = group.get("canonical_index")
273	            work_key = f"canon:{canonical_index}" if canonical_index is not None else ""
274	        members = [str(m) for m in (group.get("member_evidence_ids") or []) if str(m)]
275	        if not work_key or not members:
276	            continue
277	        for ev_id in members:
278	            alias_of.setdefault(ev_id, work_key)
279	
280	    # Item 5: normalized-DOI fold for same-DOI-different-URL copies the cp3 URL groups left split.
281	    # Rows sharing ONE usable ``10.`` DOI are the SAME underlying work even when their source URLs
282	    # differ, so ``outline_digest.py:533`` (baskets sorted by -work_count) no longer ranks a
283	    # pseudo-corroborated same-DOI basket above a genuine multi-work basket. Mirrors the item-2
284	    # title fold: a DOI group that OVERLAPS a cp3 group ADOPTS that group's key (UNIFIED into one
285	    # work); an unclaimed DOI group takes the ``doi:`` key itself. Runs BEFORE the title fold so a
286	    # DOI-unified key is the one a later title fold adopts. ``evidence=None`` => no DOI fold
287	    # (byte-identical to the cp3-only map); byte-identical too when no >=2 rows share a DOI.
288	    if evidence:
289	        doi_groups: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
290	        for row in evidence:
291	            if not isinstance(row, Mapping):
292	                continue
293	            ev_id = str(row.get("evidence_id", "") or "")
294	            if not ev_id:
295	                continue
296	            dkey = _normalize_doi(row.get("doi"))
297	            if not dkey:
298	                continue
299	            doi_groups.setdefault(f"doi:{dkey}", []).append(ev_id)
300	        for dkey, members in doi_groups.items():
301	            if len(members) < 2:
302	                continue  # a lone DOI is its own work — never fold a singleton
303	            # Item 4 false-merge guard (mirror): if TWO OR MORE distinct cp3 works carry this DOI (a
304	            # metadata error), do NOT merge them — fold only the UNCLAIMED members onto the DOI key,
305	            # leaving each stamped member on its own cp3 key. Otherwise unify the group onto the one
306	            # cp3 key it overlaps (or the ``doi:`` key when the cp3 groups never touched it).
307	            stamped = {alias_of[e] for e in members if e in alias_of}
308	            if len(stamped) >= 2:
309	                # Observability tripwire: this REFUSAL to merge two distinct cp3 works that share one
310	                # DOI is empirically zero-incidence on the real corpus. Count it so a future corpus
311	                # that DOES hit it is visible (never silent — see OutlineDigestMenu.doi_false_merge...).
312	                if stats is not None:
313	                    stats["doi_false_merge_guard_hits"] = stats.get("doi_false_merge_guard_hits", 0) + 1
314	                for ev_id in members:
315	                    if ev_id not in alias_of:
316	                        alias_of[ev_id] = dkey
317	            else:
318	                canonical_key = next(iter(stamped)) if stamped else dkey
319	                for ev_id in members:
320	                    alias_of[ev_id] = canonical_key
321	
322	    # Item 2: normalized-TITLE fallback fold for TITLE-identical works the cp3 groups missed.
323	    if evidence:
324	        title_groups: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
325	        # Item 3b guard: the set of title keys that have at least one member row whose RAW title was
326	        # TRUNCATED (ended with "..."/"…" BEFORE _strip_title_chrome peeled it). Only a truncated key
327	        # may PREFIX-fold onto a longer full-title key — a non-truncated short title that merely happens
328	        # to be a prefix of a longer distinct work (ev_044 "Artificial Intelligence and the Labor
329	        # Market" vs ev_073 "...- Sciences Po") must NOT false-merge. §-1.3: a WRONG fold misstates
330	        # corroboration, so the prefix fold now requires the truncation signal.
331	        truncated_keys: set[str] = set()
332	        for row in evidence:
333	            if not isinstance(row, Mapping):
334	                continue
335	            ev_id = str(row.get("evidence_id", "") or "")
336	            if not ev_id:
337	                continue
338	            _raw_title = str(row.get("title", "") or "").strip()
339	            tkey = _normalized_title_key(_raw_title)
340	            if tkey is None:
341	                continue
342	            title_groups.setdefault(tkey, []).append(ev_id)
343	            if _raw_title.endswith("...") or _raw_title.endswith("…"):
344	                truncated_keys.add(tkey)
345	
346	        # Item 3b (PREFIX FOLD): a TRUNCATED title keys to a PREFIX of the full title's key
347	        # ("...Impact ..." truncations vs the full paper title — ev_884/ev_890 vs ev_886/ev_882).
348	        # Fold each key onto the shortest STRICTLY-longer key it is a full prefix of, so the
349	        # truncated rows unify with the full-title rows. Every key here is already
350	        # >= _TITLE_FALLBACK_MIN_CHARS alnum chars (the floor), so an exact-prefix match on 25+
351	        # identical leading chars is a strong same-work signal — Fable: keep the 25-char floor so
352	        # distinct works never merge. Deterministic (length then lexicographic).
353	        fold_target: dict[str, str] = {}
354	        _keys_by_len = sorted(title_groups.keys(), key=lambda k: (len(k), k))
355	        for _i, _short in enumerate(_keys_by_len):
356	            # Item 3b guard: only a TRUNCATED short key may fold onto a longer full-title key. A short
357	            # key that is merely a prefix of a longer DISTINCT work (never truncated) stays its own
358	            # work — this is the ev_044/ev_073 false-merge fix. Non-truncated identical titles still
359	            # fold by EXACT key equality (same title_groups bucket, untouched by this prefix pass).
360	            if _short not in truncated_keys:
361	                continue
362	            for _long in _keys_by_len[_i + 1:]:
363	                if len(_long) > len(_short) and _long.startswith(_short):
364	                    fold_target[_short] = _long
365	                    break
366	
367	        def _final_key(k: str) -> str:
368	            _seen: set[str] = set()
369	            while k in fold_target and k not in _seen:
370	                _seen.add(k)
371	                k = fold_target[k]
372	            return k
373	
374	        merged_groups: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
375	        for _k, _members in title_groups.items():
376	            merged_groups.setdefault(_final_key(_k), []).extend(_members)
377	
378	        # Item 4b (TRUNCATION-VETTED SAME-WORK EXEMPTION — the CESifo WP 10601 fold): a merged group
379	        # whose final key ABSORBED a truncated prefix key was assembled by the item-3b prefix fold,
380	        # which ALREADY ruled those title buckets one work (truncated raw title + exact 25+ char
381	        # prefix — a strong same-work signal). When cp3 mistakenly split that one work into TWO
382	        # ``titlealone:`` groups (a full-title group and a truncated ``[PDF] … on …`` group, e.g.
383	        # "The Short-Term Effects of Generative Artificial Intelligence on Employment" = CESifo WP
384	        # 10601), the downstream item-4 guard would see >=2 cp3 keys and REFUSE to merge — miscounting
385	        # ONE work as TWO in the ``-work_count`` basket ranking. So for these prefix-folded finals we
386	        # DEFER to item 3b and unify, canonicalizing onto the FULL-title bucket's cp3 key. The
387	        # exact-title collision guard (no truncation, e.g. ev_044/ev_073, the item-4 test) is
388	        # UNAFFECTED: those finals never absorbed a truncated prefix, so they still hit the refusal.
389	        prefix_folded_finals = {_final_key(_k) for _k in fold_target}
390	
391	        for tkey, members in merged_groups.items():
392	            if len(members) < 2:
393	                continue  # a unique title is its own work — never fold a lone title
394	            # Item 4 (ALIAS-MAP FALSE-MERGE GUARD): collect the DISTINCT cp3 work keys the members
395	            # already carry. If TWO OR MORE different cp3 works share this normalized title, do NOT
396	            # false-merge them onto one key — leave each stamped member on its OWN cp3 key and fold
397	            # only the UNCLAIMED (title-only) members onto the title key. Only when the group carries
398	            # exactly ONE (or zero) cp3 key do we unify the whole group onto it. §-1.3: a fold never
399	            # drops a row; a WRONG fold would misstate corroboration, so the guard stays conservative.
400	            stamped = {alias_of[e] for e in members if e in alias_of}
401	            if len(stamped) >= 2 and tkey not in prefix_folded_finals:
402	                if stats is not None:
403	                    stats["title_false_merge_guard_hits"] = stats.get("title_false_merge_guard_hits", 0) + 1
404	                for ev_id in members:
405	                    if ev_id not in alias_of:
406	                        alias_of[ev_id] = tkey
407	            elif len(stamped) >= 2:
408	                # Item 4b: truncation-vetted same work — canonicalize onto the FULL-title bucket's cp3
409	                # key (the members whose OWN normalized title is this final target key, i.e. the rows
410	                # that were NOT truncated-prefix-folded in). Deterministic (sorted). Collapses the two
411	                # cp3 titlealone groups to ONE work so the work count is correct.
412	                _target_members = title_groups.get(tkey, [])
413	                _target_keys = sorted({alias_of[e] for e in _target_members if e in alias_of})
414	                canonical_key = _target_keys[0] if _target_keys else sorted(stamped)[0]
415	                for ev_id in members:
416	                    alias_of[ev_id] = canonical_key
417	            else:
418	                canonical_key = next(iter(stamped)) if stamped else tkey
419	                for ev_id in members:
420	                    alias_of[ev_id] = canonical_key
421	    return alias_of

424	# Item 6c (§-1.3.1(a) content-integrity, NOT a lexical quality guess): a row whose TITLE is a known
425	# fetch INTERSTITIAL is a failed fetch surfaced as a Cloudflare/bot/404 page — not a source. The row
426	# is KEPT (disclosure), but TAGGED so the planner never anchors on it. Exact/known-prefix match only.
427	_CHROME_INTERSTITIAL_TAG = " [CHROME — failed fetch, do not anchor]"
428	_CHROME_INTERSTITIAL_PREFIXES = (
429	    "just a moment",
430	    "attention required",
431	    "access denied",
432	    "verifying you are human",
433	    "are you a robot",
434	    "one moment, please",
435	    "please wait",
436	    "checking your browser",
437	)
438	_CHROME_INTERSTITIAL_EXACT = frozenset({
439	    "404", "404 not found", "404: not found", "page not found",
440	    "403 forbidden", "error 404", "error 403",
441	})
442	
443	
444	def _is_chrome_interstitial(title: str) -> bool:
445	    """True when a row TITLE is a known fetch interstitial (Cloudflare "Just a moment...", "Access
446	    Denied", a bare "404", "Attention Required"). Exact / known-prefix match only — content-integrity
447	    under §-1.3.1(a), NEVER a lexical quality judgement. Conservative: an unknown title => ``False``
448	    (KEEP + present normally; the tag only fires on a confirmed interstitial string)."""
449	    t = (title or "").strip().lower().rstrip(" .!")
450	    if not t:
451	        return False
452	    if any(t.startswith(p) for p in _CHROME_INTERSTITIAL_PREFIXES):
453	        return True
454	    return t in _CHROME_INTERSTITIAL_EXACT
455

457	# Item 4 (§-1.3 principle 1 — WEIGHT surfaced, never a drop): a row the topic judge WEIGHT-demoted for
458	# relevance (topic_offtopic_demoted, or content_relevance_label in {demoted, escalated_demoted}) is
459	# KEPT and presented, but marked so the planner PREFERS non-demoted sources when both support a claim.
460	# This is GUIDANCE only — never a filter. The stamp fields ride onto the bank row from cp2 (build_bank).
461	_DEMOTED_WEIGHT_TAG = " [w:demoted]"
462	_DEMOTED_LABELS = frozenset({"demoted", "escalated_demoted"})
463	
464	
465	def _is_weight_demoted(row: Mapping[str, Any]) -> bool:
466	    """True when a bank row carries a relevance WEIGHT-demote stamp (item 4). Surfaced to the planner
467	    as a compact ``[w:demoted]`` marker — §-1.3 principle 1: the weight is DISCLOSED, never a drop.
468	    A missing stamp => not demoted (present normally). Never raises on a malformed row (fail-open)."""
469	    if not isinstance(row, Mapping):
470	        return False
471	    if row.get("topic_offtopic_demoted"):
472	        return True
473	    return str(row.get("content_relevance_label", "") or "").strip().lower() in _DEMOTED_LABELS

476	def _basket_line(
477	    bid: str,
478	    corroboration: int,
479	    tiers: list[str],
480	    claim: str,
481	    member_ev_ids: list[str],
482	    *,
483	    elide_members: bool,
484	    work_corroboration: int | None = None,
485	    row_count: int | None = None,
486	    chrome: bool = False,
487	    demoted: bool = False,
488	) -> str:
489	    tier_csv = ",".join(tiers)
490	    members = (
491	        f"({len(member_ev_ids)} members)"
492	        if elide_members
493	        else "members: " + ",".join(member_ev_ids)
494	    )
495	    # PUSH A: when work-level corroboration is known, render the HONEST count — how many distinct
496	    # WORKS (not rows) back the claim — while still listing every member row (aliases stay).
497	    # ``work_corroboration is None`` (no same_work_groups) => the legacy ``xN sources`` head =>
498	    # byte-identical.
499	    if work_corroboration is None:
500	        head = f"[x{corroboration} sources: {tier_csv}]"
501	    else:
502	        head = f"[x{work_corroboration} works ({row_count} rows): {tier_csv}]"
503	    # item 6c: a basket built from a fetch-interstitial title (32 "Just a moment..." Cloudflare rows
504	    # consolidated into whole baskets this run) is tagged so the planner does not anchor on it. Kept
505	    # (disclosure), just not sold as evidence (§-1.3.1(a) content-integrity).
506	    chrome_tag = _CHROME_INTERSTITIAL_TAG if chrome else ""
507	    # item 4: a WEIGHT-demoted basket (its representative row demoted for relevance) is marked after
508	    # the head so the planner prefers non-demoted baskets when both support a claim (§-1.3 principle 1
509	    # — weight surfaced, never dropped). ``demoted=False`` (default) => "" => byte-identical.
510	    demoted_tag = _DEMOTED_WEIGHT_TAG if demoted else ""
511	    return f'{bid} {head}{demoted_tag}{chrome_tag} claim: "{claim}" {members}'

514	def _singleton_line(
515	    ev_id: str, tier: str, title: str, statement: str, alias_ev_ids: list[str],
516	    *, title_only: bool, seminal: bool = False, chrome: bool = False, demoted: bool = False,
517	) -> str:
518	    # PUSH A: same-work copies collapsed onto this line are disclosed inline after the tier;
519	    # empty ``alias_ev_ids`` (the no-same_work_groups path) => no tag => byte-identical.
520	    tag = f" (+{len(alias_ev_ids)} same-work: {','.join(alias_ev_ids)})" if alias_ev_ids else ""
521	    # Item 4a: an explicit seminal marker on a T1 singleton (the tier tag alone reads as one more
522	    # row in an 58k-char menu). Placed AFTER the ev_id token so ``covered_ev_ids`` still parses the
523	    # head. ``seminal=False`` (default) => "" => byte-identical.
524	    seminal_tag = " [seminal T1 — consider for anchoring]" if seminal else ""
525	    # item 6c: a singleton whose title is a known fetch interstitial (Cloudflare "Just a moment...",
526	    # "Access Denied", "404") is tagged — kept for disclosure, but never sold to the planner as an
527	    # anchorable source (§-1.3.1(a) content-integrity). Placed after the tier so ``covered_ev_ids``
528	    # still parses the ev_id head token. ``chrome=False`` (default) => "" => byte-identical.
529	    chrome_tag = _CHROME_INTERSTITIAL_TAG if chrome else ""
530	    # item 4: a WEIGHT-demoted singleton (demoted for relevance) is marked right after the tier so the
531	    # planner prefers non-demoted sources when both support a claim (§-1.3 principle 1 — weight
532	    # surfaced, never a drop). Placed after ``[{tier}]`` so ``covered_ev_ids`` still parses the ev_id
533	    # head token. ``demoted=False`` (default) => "" => byte-identical.
534	    demoted_tag = _DEMOTED_WEIGHT_TAG if demoted else ""
535	    if title and statement and not title_only:
536	        return f"{ev_id} [{tier}]{demoted_tag}{chrome_tag}{seminal_tag}{tag} | title: {title} | {statement}"
537	    if title:
538	        return f"{ev_id} [{tier}]{demoted_tag}{chrome_tag}{seminal_tag}{tag} | title: {title}"
539	    return f"{ev_id} [{tier}]{demoted_tag}{chrome_tag}{seminal_tag}{tag}: {statement}"

542	def _is_title_like(statement: str, title: str) -> bool:
543	    """PUSH 4 (S4-local title-like mitigation): True when a candidate basket claim is really a
544	    bare TITLE, not a claim sentence — an S3 representative_statement that fell back to the row
545	    title (measured title-like on a large fraction of cp3 baskets). Detected deterministically:
546	    empty; a ``[PDF]`` / ``(PDF)`` chrome prefix; a truncated ``...`` tail; or byte-equal to the
547	    row title. This is a LOCAL display mitigation (pick a better member statement), never a drop
548	    — the upstream fix (S3 should emit a claim sentence) is filed as a separate note."""
549	    s = (statement or "").strip()
550	    if not s:
551	        return True
552	    low = s.lower()
553	    # item 10: also treat a "URL Source:" chrome prefix and a unicode-ellipsis "…" truncation tail
554	    # as title-like (the ascii "..." tail + "[pdf]"/"(pdf)" prefixes were already covered).
555	    if low.startswith("[pdf]") or low.startswith("(pdf)") or low.startswith("url source:"):
556	        return True
557	    if s.endswith("...") or s.endswith("…"):
558	        return True
559	    if title and s == title.strip():
560	        return True
561	    return False
