1	"""Additive overstatement guards for the faithfulness engine (I-deepfix B16, #1360).
2	
3	Basket B16 root cause: a generator paraphrase can UPGRADE the epistemic status
4	of a cited span (a stated modeling ASSUMPTION — "we assumed 60%" — re-rendered
5	as an empirical "estimate"/"finding") or SILENTLY WIDEN/MERGE a time-horizon
6	(a source's "over five years" reported under a different "2010-2023" window)
7	while the NUMBERS still match — so strict_verify's numeric leg passes and the
8	overstatement survives.
9	
10	These are **ADDITIVE** legs layered on top of `strict_verify` /
11	`verify_sentence_provenance`. They only ever ADD a failure reason (a drop) or a
12	soft warning; they NEVER clear an existing failure and NEVER relax any existing
13	check. Both legs are pure, stdlib-only, network-free leaf functions so they sit
14	on the hot per-sentence path with zero cost; both are env-flag gated so the
15	default behaviour is preserved byte-for-byte when disabled.
16	
17	Design (mirrors the existing `shell_detector` / `_detect_unhedged_superlative`
18	additive patterns in `provenance_generator`):
19	
20	  1. EPISTEMIC-MARKER guard — if the cited span carries an explicit modeling /
21	     projection hedge (``we assumed``, ``projected``, ``modeled``, ``hypothetical
22	     scenario``, ``for illustration``...) then the generated paraphrase MUST
23	     carry an epistemic hedge too. A paraphrase that converts the assumption
24	     into a bare empirical assertion ("the estimate is 60%", "data show 60%")
25	     drops. A paraphrase that preserves ANY hedge (its own assumption-marker, an
26	     attribution like "the model assumes", or a generic uncertainty hedge such
27	     as "may"/"could") passes.
28	
29	  2. TEMPORAL-SCOPE guard — when BOTH the claim sentence AND the cited span name
30	     an explicit time-horizon (an "over N years" duration OR a "YYYY-YYYY" year
31	     range), the claim's horizon must be REPRESENTED in the cited span. A claim
32	     that asserts a duration / year-range that the span does not contain (the
33	     "5-year per-firm effect" merged into a "2010-2023 window", or a widened
34	     duration) drops. If the span names no horizon, or the claim names none, the
35	     guard is inert (no false drop — the numeric/content legs already govern).
36	"""
37	
38	from __future__ import annotations
39	
40	import os
41	import re
42	
43	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
44	# Env flags (LAW VI — named, env-gated, no magic numbers).
45	# Default ON: these are faithfulness-TIGHTENING (they only add drops/flags).
46	# Setting the flag to a falsy value reverts the relevant leg to byte-identical
47	# pre-B16 behaviour.
48	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
49	_TRUE_TOKENS = frozenset({"1", "true", "yes", "on", "enforce", "warn"})
50	
51	
52	def epistemic_guard_enabled() -> bool:
53	    """Whether the epistemic-marker preservation leg is active (default ON)."""
54	    return os.getenv("PG_EPISTEMIC_MARKER_GUARD", "1").strip().lower() in _TRUE_TOKENS
55	
56	
57	def temporal_scope_guard_enabled() -> bool:
58	    """Whether the temporal-scope-match leg is active (default ON)."""
59	    return os.getenv("PG_TEMPORAL_SCOPE_GUARD", "1").strip().lower() in _TRUE_TOKENS
60	
61	
62	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
63	# Epistemic markers
64	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
65	# A span that carries one of these is making a MODELING ASSUMPTION / PROJECTION,
66	# not reporting an empirical observation. The verb is the strong signal; a plain
67	# "estimate" alone is NOT here (a published point estimate is empirical) — we
68	# require the assumption/projection framing.
69	# NARROWED to VALUE-LEVEL assumption/projection framing only (I-deepfix-001 Codex
70	# P1, iter 2): the bare nouns "assumption(s)" and the bare verb "model(ed/ing)"
71	# match EMPIRICAL statistical-method prose — "the proportional hazards
72	# assumption", "the normality assumption", "outcomes were modeled using logistic
73	# regression", "we modeled survival via Cox regression" — none of which render a
74	# value as a finding. Matching those default-ON would drop faithful empirical
75	# claims (breadth loss). The triggers below require an explicit assumption /
76	# projection / hypothetical / simulation FRAMING tied to a stated value, not the
77	# mere mention of the word "assumption" or "model" in a methods description.
78	_SPAN_ASSUMPTION_RE = re.compile(
79	    r"(?:"
80	    r"\bwe\s+assume(?:d|s)?\b"
81	    r"|\bif\s+we\s+assume\b"
82	    r"|\bunder\s+(?:the|an?)\s+assumption\b"
83	    r"|\bassuming\s+(?:a|an|the|that)\b"
84	    r"|\bwe\s+project(?:ed|s)?\b"
85	    r"|\bprojected\s+to\b"
86	    r"|\bhypothes(?:is|ise[ds]?|ize[ds]?|ised|ized)\b"
87	    r"|\bhypothetical\b"
88	    r"|\billustrative\b"
89	    r"|\bfor\s+illustration\b"
90	    r"|\bscenario\s+analysis\b"
91	    r"|\bsimulat(?:e|es|ed|ion|ions)\b"
92	    r")",
93	    re.IGNORECASE,
94	)
95	
96	# A claim sentence preserves the epistemic status if it carries EITHER an
97	# explicit assumption/projection marker of its own OR a generic uncertainty
98	# hedge that does not over-assert empirical certainty.
99	_CLAIM_HEDGE_RE = re.compile(
100	    r"\b("
101	    r"assum(?:e|es|ed|ing|ption|ptions)"
102	    r"|project(?:ed|s|ion|ions)?"
103	    r"|model(?:ed|led|s|ing|ling)?"
104	    r"|hypothes(?:is|ise[ds]?|ize[ds]?|ised|ized)"
105	    r"|hypothetical(?:ly)?"
106	    r"|illustrative(?:ly)?"
107	    r"|scenario"
108	    r"|simulat(?:e|es|ed|ion|ions)"
109	    r"|estimat(?:e|es|ed|ion)"   # an "estimate" framing keeps the modeled status (vs a bare assertion)
110	    r"|may|might|could|would"
111	    r"|expect(?:ed|s)?"
112	    r"|forecast(?:ed|s)?"
113	    r"|predict(?:ed|s|ion|ions)?"
114	    r"|theoretical(?:ly)?"
115	    r")\b",
116	    re.IGNORECASE,
117	)
118	
119	# Empirical-certainty verbs that, with NO hedge, STATE an assumption as a finding.
120	# Used only to confirm the paraphrase is making a bare assertion (precision: a
121	# span hedge + claim with one of these AND no claim hedge = overstatement).
122	_CLAIM_EMPIRICAL_RE = re.compile(
123	    r"\b("
124	    r"found|finds|showed|shows|demonstrat(?:ed|es)"
125	    r"|observ(?:ed|es)|report(?:ed|s)?|reveal(?:ed|s)?"
126	    r"|data\s+show|results?\s+show|the\s+(?:finding|evidence)"
127	    r"|is|are|was|were|has|have|will\s+(?:add|create|generate|produce)"
128	    r")\b",
129	    re.IGNORECASE,
130	)
131	
132	# A number token (digits, optional decimal) used to anchor a span's assumed VALUE
133	# and to confirm the claim reports THAT value as a finding.
134	_NUMERIC_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"\d+(?:\.\d+)?")
135	# Clause boundary: an assumption marker only governs up to the next break, so a
136	# number AFTER the break (a downstream RESULT) is not the assumed value. A period
137	# between digits is a decimal point, NOT a boundary.
138	_CLAUSE_BOUNDARY_RE = re.compile(r"[,;:]|\.(?!\d)")
139	
140	
141	def _assumed_values_in_span(span_text: str) -> set[str]:
142	    """Number tokens the span frames as ASSUMED / PROJECTED VALUES.
143	
144	    For each assumption/projection marker, collect the numbers that appear in the
145	    SAME clause (from the marker to the next clause boundary). Returns empty when a
146	    marker governs a non-numeric methodological condition ("assuming that censoring
147	    was non-informative", "under the assumption of proportional hazards") — so the
148	    epistemic leg stays INERT on empirical statistical-method prose (I-deepfix-001
149	    Codex P1).
150	    """
151	    out: set[str] = set()
152	    for m in _SPAN_ASSUMPTION_RE.finditer(span_text or ""):
153	        tail = span_text[m.end():]
154	        boundary = _CLAUSE_BOUNDARY_RE.search(tail)
155	        clause = tail[: boundary.start()] if boundary else tail
156	        out.update(_NUMERIC_TOKEN_RE.findall(clause))
157	    return out
158	
159	
160	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
161	# Temporal horizons
162	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
163	# Duration windows: "over five years", "over 5 years", "within 10 years",
164	# "a 5-year period", "5-year".  Captures the numeral (word or digit) + unit.
165	_NUMWORD = {
166	    "one": 1, "two": 2, "three": 3, "four": 4, "five": 5,
167	    "six": 6, "seven": 7, "eight": 8, "nine": 9, "ten": 10,
168	    "eleven": 11, "twelve": 12, "thirteen": 13, "fourteen": 14, "fifteen": 15,
169	    "twenty": 20, "thirty": 30, "forty": 40, "fifty": 50,
170	}
171	_DURATION_RE = re.compile(
172	    r"\b(\d{1,3}|one|two|three|four|five|six|seven|eight|nine|ten|"
173	    r"eleven|twelve|thirteen|fourteen|fifteen|twenty|thirty|forty|fifty)"
174	    r"[\s-]+"
175	    r"(year|yr|month|week|day|decade)s?\b",
176	    re.IGNORECASE,
177	)
178	# Explicit calendar year ranges: "2010-2023", "2010 to 2023", "2010–2023".
179	_YEAR_RANGE_RE = re.compile(
180	    r"\b(19\d{2}|20\d{2})\s*(?:-|–|—|to|through|–)\s*(19\d{2}|20\d{2})\b",
181	    re.IGNORECASE,
182	)
183	
184	
185	def _normalize_number_word(tok: str) -> int | None:
186	    tok = tok.strip().lower()
187	    if tok.isdigit():
188	        return int(tok)
189	    return _NUMWORD.get(tok)
190	
191	
192	def _durations_in(text: str) -> set[tuple[int, str]]:
193	    """Set of (count, unit-singular) durations named in `text`."""
194	    out: set[tuple[int, str]] = set()
195	    for m in _DURATION_RE.finditer(text or ""):
196	        n = _normalize_number_word(m.group(1))
197	        if n is None:
198	            continue
199	        unit = m.group(2).lower().rstrip("s")
200	        if unit == "yr":
201	            unit = "year"
202	        out.add((n, unit))
203	    return out
204	
205	
206	def _year_ranges_in(text: str) -> set[tuple[int, int]]:
207	    """Set of (start_year, end_year) calendar ranges named in `text`."""
208	    out: set[tuple[int, int]] = set()
209	    for m in _YEAR_RANGE_RE.finditer(text or ""):
210	        a, b = int(m.group(1)), int(m.group(2))
211	        if a <= b:
212	            out.add((a, b))
213	    return out
214	
215	
216	# Canonical magnitude (in days) for each duration unit, so equivalent horizons
217	# expressed in different units compare EQUAL: 12 months == 52 weeks == 1 year.
218	# (LAW VI: named constants, not magic numbers in the comparison.)
219	_DAYS_PER_YEAR = 365.0
220	_DAYS_PER_UNIT = {
221	    "day": 1.0,
222	    "week": 7.0,
223	    "month": _DAYS_PER_YEAR / 12.0,
224	    "year": _DAYS_PER_YEAR,
225	    "decade": _DAYS_PER_YEAR * 10.0,
226	}
227	# A claim horizon counts as a WIDENING only if its magnitude exceeds the span's
228	# LONGEST horizon by more than this relative margin. Generous on purpose: the
229	# guard's overriding constraint is to NEVER false-drop a legitimately-scoped
230	# claim — unit phrasing ("12 months" vs "one year"), rounding ("52 weeks" vs
231	# "one year"), and a NARROWER claim horizon must all pass; the leg fires only on
232	# a genuine scope-widening beyond the cited evidence.
233	_TEMPORAL_WIDEN_REL_TOLERANCE = 0.25
234	
235	
236	def _duration_days(count: int, unit: str) -> float:
237	    return float(count) * _DAYS_PER_UNIT.get(unit, _DAYS_PER_YEAR)
238	
239	
240	def _range_days(start_year: int, end_year: int) -> float:
241	    return float(end_year - start_year) * _DAYS_PER_YEAR
242	
243	
244	def _max_horizon_days(
245	    durs: set[tuple[int, str]], ranges: set[tuple[int, int]]
246	) -> float:
247	    """Longest horizon (in days) named in a text; 0.0 if none."""
248	    candidates = [_duration_days(n, u) for n, u in durs]
249	    candidates += [_range_days(a, b) for a, b in ranges]
250	    return max(candidates) if candidates else 0.0
251	
252	
253	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
254	# Public leg API.  Each returns a failure-reason string (drop) or None (pass).
255	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
256	def epistemic_overstatement_reason(claim: str, span_text: str) -> str | None:
257	    """Return a drop reason if the paraphrase strips the span's epistemic hedge.
258	
259	    Fires only when:
260	      - the cited span explicitly frames the value as an ASSUMPTION/PROJECTION,
261	      - AND the claim makes a bare empirical assertion (an empirical verb),
262	      - AND the claim carries NO hedge of its own.
263	    Otherwise returns None (inert — no false drop).
264	    """
265	    if not claim or not span_text:
266	        return None
267	    # VALUE-ANCHORED (I-deepfix-001 Codex P1, iter 3->4): fire ONLY when the span
268	    # frames a specific VALUE as assumed/projected (a number inside the assumption
269	    # marker's own clause) AND the claim asserts that SAME value as a finding. A
270	    # methodological caveat governing a non-numeric condition ("assuming that
271	    # censoring was non-informative", "under the assumption of proportional
272	    # hazards") carries no in-clause number -> inert (no false drop on methods/
273	    # results prose). A claim that does not echo the assumed value -> inert.
274	    assumed_values = _assumed_values_in_span(span_text)
275	    if not assumed_values:
276	        return None  # no value-level assumption framing
277	    if _CLAIM_HEDGE_RE.search(claim):
278	        return None  # paraphrase preserved an epistemic hedge — faithful
279	    if not _CLAIM_EMPIRICAL_RE.search(claim):
280	        return None  # claim isn't asserting empirically; leave to other legs
281	    claim_numbers = set(_NUMERIC_TOKEN_RE.findall(claim))
282	    if not (assumed_values & claim_numbers):
283	        return None  # the claim does not report the assumed value as a finding
284	    return "epistemic_overstatement_assumption_as_finding"
285	
286	
287	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
288	# Effect-size conditional/threshold guard (WS-5 FIX-b, I-deepfix-001 beat-both).
289	#
290	# The re-smoke residual D1: the Eloundou span reads
291	#   "...roughly 1.8% of jobs could have over half their tasks affected by LLMs...
292	#    When accounting for current and likely future software developments that
293	#    complement LLM capabilities, this share jumps to just over 46% of jobs."
294	# so the "46%" figure is GOVERNED by the antecedent "when accounting for future
295	# software developments" (a conditional) and the threshold "over half their tasks
296	# affected". A re-lift that renders "just over 46% of jobs are exposed to
297	# LLM-related technologies" keeps the NUMBER (strict_verify's numeric leg passes)
298	# but DROPS the governing condition — an effect-size over-claim.
299	#
300	# This leg is ANNOTATE-ONLY (§-1.3): it returns a reason so the render layer can
301	# APPEND a [confidence] caveat (or so the composer can carry the antecedent). It
302	# NEVER drops a sentence, NEVER widens a span, NEVER changes a verdict. It is NOT
303	# wired into strict_verify's drop path. Gated by PG_FIGURE_CONSISTENCY_ANNOTATE
304	# (default ON); OFF -> inert (byte-identical). Pure, stdlib-only, network-free.
305	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
306	def figure_consistency_annotate_enabled() -> bool:
307	    """Whether the effect-size conditional/threshold annotate leg is active (default ON)."""
308	    return os.getenv("PG_FIGURE_CONSISTENCY_ANNOTATE", "1").strip().lower() in _TRUE_TOKENS
309	
310	
311	# Provenance / numbered-citation markers stripped off the CLAIM before number
312	# extraction, so a span offset inside "[#ev:eloundou:0-800]" or a "[7]" marker is
313	# never mistaken for a claimed figure.
314	_CLAIM_CITATION_STRIP_RE = re.compile(r"\[#ev:[^\]]+\]|\[\d+\]")
315	
316	# A governing conditional / threshold token in the SPAN. When one of these
317	# precedes a number in the span's number-bearing sentence, that number is
318	# CONDITIONAL (its magnitude holds only under the stated antecedent/threshold),
319	# not an unconditional empirical count. The task's three canonical cues
320	# ("when accounting for", "could have", "over half") lead the set; the rest are
321	# the standard conditional/threshold/scenario framings.
322	_SPAN_EFFECT_CONDITION_RE = re.compile(
323	    r"(?:"
324	    r"when\s+accounting\s+for"
325	    r"|when\s+we\s+account\s+for"
326	    r"|\bcould\s+have\b"
327	    r"|\bover\s+half\b"
328	    r"|\bup\s+to\b"
329	    r"|\bas\s+(?:much|many)\s+as\b"
330	    r"|\bassum(?:e|es|ed|ing)\b"
331	    r"|\bif\s+(?:we|you|the|current|future)\b"
332	    r"|\bprovided\s+that\b"
333	    r"|\bunder\s+(?:the|a|an)\s+\w+\s+scenario\b"
334	    r"|\bhypothetical(?:ly)?\b"
335	    r"|\bscenario\b"
336	    r")",
337	    re.IGNORECASE,
338	)
339	
340	# A governing condition/hedge CARRIED BY THE CLAIM. If the claim reproduces any
341	# of these, the antecedent travelled with the number (the paraphrase is faithful)
342	# -> the leg is inert (no caveat). Deliberately EXCLUDES bare epistemic verbs like
343	# "estimate"/"found" (those keep the value framed as a finding, NOT as conditional)
344	# so a bare re-lift such as "they estimate that just over 46% of jobs..." still
345	# fires; it targets the specific conditional/threshold antecedent only.
346	_CLAIM_ANTECEDENT_CARRIED_RE = re.compile(
347	    r"(?:"
348	    r"account(?:ing|s|ed)?\s+for"
349	    r"|\bcould\b|\bmay\b|\bmight\b|\bwould\b"
350	    r"|\bup\s+to\b|\bas\s+(?:much|many)\s+as\b|\bover\s+half\b"
351	    r"|\bif\b|\bassum(?:e|es|ed|ing)\b|\bprovided\b"
352	    r"|\bscenario\b|\bhypothetical(?:ly)?\b"
353	    r"|\bwhen\b"
354	    r"|future\s+(?:software|developments|technolog)"
355	    r")",
356	    re.IGNORECASE,
357	)
358	
359	# Split the span into sentences so a conditional only governs numbers in its OWN
360	# sentence (a period that is NOT a decimal point is a boundary).
361	_SPAN_SENTENCE_SPLIT_RE = re.compile(r"(?<=[.!?])\s+")
362	
363	
364	def _effect_size_conditional_core(claim: str, span_text: str) -> str | None:
365	    """FLAG-FREE detection core (shared by the ANNOTATE leg and the #9 DROP leg): return
366	    the shared-number reason iff the claim re-lifts a span number while dropping the
367	    conditional/threshold antecedent that governs it in the cited span; else None. Pure."""
368	    if not claim or not span_text:
369	        return None
370	    claim_bare = _CLAIM_CITATION_STRIP_RE.sub("", claim)
371	    claim_numbers = set(_NUMERIC_TOKEN_RE.findall(claim_bare))
372	    if not claim_numbers:
373	        return None  # no figure re-lifted — nothing to over-claim
374	    if _CLAIM_ANTECEDENT_CARRIED_RE.search(claim_bare):
375	        return None  # the claim carries the governing condition — antecedent travelled, faithful
376	    for sentence in _SPAN_SENTENCE_SPLIT_RE.split(span_text):
377	        cond = _SPAN_EFFECT_CONDITION_RE.search(sentence)
378	        if not cond:
379	            continue
380	        # Numbers AFTER the conditional in this sentence are the ones it governs.
381	        governed = set(_NUMERIC_TOKEN_RE.findall(sentence[cond.start():]))
382	        shared = claim_numbers & governed
383	        if shared:
384	            return ",".join(sorted(shared))
385	    return None
386	
387	
388	def effect_size_conditional_reason(claim: str, span_text: str) -> str | None:
389	    """Return an ANNOTATE reason if the claim re-lifts a span number while dropping
390	    the conditional/threshold antecedent that governs it in the cited span.
391	
392	    Fires only when ALL hold:
393	      - the leg is enabled (PG_FIGURE_CONSISTENCY_ANNOTATE, default ON),
394	      - the claim carries NO governing conditional/threshold of its own (a bare
395	        assertion — "they estimate that just over 46% of jobs are exposed..."),
396	      - the cited span has a sentence whose governing conditional/threshold token
397	        PRECEDES a number, and
398	      - the claim reproduces that governed number.
399	    Otherwise returns None (inert — no false caveat). ANNOTATE-only: the caller
400	    APPENDS a [confidence] caveat; this NEVER drops, widens a span, or changes a
401	    verdict (§-1.3).
402	    """
403	    if not figure_consistency_annotate_enabled():
404	        return None
405	    shared = _effect_size_conditional_core(claim, span_text)
406	    return None if shared is None else "effect_size_conditional_stripped:num=" + shared
407

409	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
410	# I-deepfix-001 B4-render #9 (#1344): tasks-vs-jobs UNIT-CONFLATION guard.
411	#
412	# The drb_72 residual #9 headline "just over 46% of jobs" misstates a share of TASKS as a share of
413	# JOBS. The existing S5 leg (``numeric_qualifier_retention_reason``) already DROPS the companion
414	# defect — a headline number re-lifted while its span-bound conditional/threshold qualifier is
415	# stripped — so the conditional-strip class is covered. This leg closes the ORTHOGONAL gap S5 does
416	# NOT own: a claim that binds a percentage to a COUNTABLE-UNIT noun (jobs / workers / occupations /
417	# positions) that the cited span binds the SAME percentage to a DIFFERENT unit (tasks / activities /
418	# work), and the span does NOT also state that percentage for the claim's unit. That is a units
419	# MISSTATEMENT ("46% of tasks" rendered as "46% of jobs"), not a number mismatch, so strict_verify's
420	# numeric leg passes it. HIGH-PRECISION: it fires only on the exact-same percentage value attached
421	# to genuinely different measure nouns, so a claim whose unit the span DOES support is inert.
422	# ADDITIVE faithfulness-TIGHTENING: APPENDS a drop; never clears a failure, never widens a span,
423	# drops NO source. LAW VI kill-switch ``PG_UNIT_CONFLATION_GUARD``; OFF reverts byte-identically.
424	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
425	def unit_conflation_guard_enabled() -> bool:
426	    """Whether the tasks-vs-jobs unit-conflation drop leg is active (default ON)."""
427	    return os.getenv("PG_UNIT_CONFLATION_GUARD", "1").strip().lower() in _TRUE_TOKENS
428	
429	
430	# The two measure-unit families that get conflated. A "job unit" counts positions/people; a "task
431	# unit" counts activities. The same percentage attached to one in the claim and the OTHER in the
432	# span (and never the claim's unit) is a units misstatement.
433	_JOB_UNIT_RE = re.compile(
434	    r"\b(?:jobs?|workers?|occupations?|positions?|employees?|roles?)\b", re.IGNORECASE,
435	)
436	_TASK_UNIT_RE = re.compile(
437	    r"\b(?:tasks?|activit(?:y|ies)|work\s+activit(?:y|ies)|duties|duty)\b", re.IGNORECASE,
438	)
439	# A percentage immediately bound to a unit noun within a short window ("46% of jobs", "46 percent of
440	# tasks"). Group 1 = the numeric string (same shape as ``_NUMERIC_TOKEN_RE``); group 2 = the unit.
441	_PCT_OF_JOB_RE = re.compile(
442	    r"(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*(?:%|percent(?:age)?|per\s*cent)\s+of\s+(?:\w+\s+){0,3}?"
443	    r"(jobs?|workers?|occupations?|positions?|employees?|roles?)\b",
444	    re.IGNORECASE,
445	)
446	_PCT_OF_TASK_RE = re.compile(
447	    r"(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*(?:%|percent(?:age)?|per\s*cent)\s+of\s+(?:\w+\s+){0,3}?"
448	    r"(tasks?|activit(?:y|ies)|duties|duty)\b",
449	    re.IGNORECASE,
450	)
451	
452	
453	def unit_conflation_reason(claim: str, span_text: str) -> str | None:
454	    """Return a DROP reason iff the leg is enabled AND the claim binds a percentage to a JOB unit
455	    that the cited span binds to a TASK unit (same value) while the span never states that
456	    percentage for a job unit — a tasks-vs-jobs units misstatement. Else None (inert). Pure,
457	    stdlib-only, network-free (mirrors the other additive B16 legs)."""
458	    if not unit_conflation_guard_enabled():
459	        return None
460	    if not claim or not span_text:
461	        return None
462	    claim_bare = _CLAIM_CITATION_STRIP_RE.sub("", claim)
463	    # Percentages the claim binds to a JOB unit.
464	    claim_job_pcts = {m.group(1) for m in _PCT_OF_JOB_RE.finditer(claim_bare)}
465	    if not claim_job_pcts:
466	        return None
467	    # Percentages the span binds to a TASK unit, and (separately) to a JOB unit.
468	    span_task_pcts = {m.group(1) for m in _PCT_OF_TASK_RE.finditer(span_text)}
469	    span_job_pcts = {m.group(1) for m in _PCT_OF_JOB_RE.finditer(span_text)}
470	    # Fire only when the SAME value is a share-of-tasks in the span but a share-of-jobs in the claim,
471	    # and the span does NOT also support that value as a share-of-jobs (then the claim's unit is OK).
472	    conflated = sorted((claim_job_pcts & span_task_pcts) - span_job_pcts)
473	    if conflated:
474	        return "unit_conflation_tasks_as_jobs:num=" + ",".join(conflated)
475	    return None

478	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
479	# One-sidedness PRIMACY advisory (I-deepfix-001 Wave-2, one-sidedness framing).
480	#
481	# A claim can headline ONE figure as the sentence's lead number while the SAME
482	# cited basket ALSO carries a materially-different COMPANION figure of the SAME
483	# measure kind that the claim omits — a primacy / one-sidedness frame. Example (the
484	# Eloundou exposure span): the claim leads with "1.8% of jobs exposed" while the
485	# basket also reports "just over 46% of tasks exposed" (the same "% of jobs/tasks
486	# exposed" measure). Surfacing only the small headline while the basket carries a
487	# far-larger same-kind companion under-states the picture.
488	#
489	# ADVISORY-ONLY (§-1.3): returns a reason so the caller APPENDS a soft-warning; it
490	# NEVER drops a sentence, NEVER fails is_verified, NEVER widens a span, NEVER
491	# changes a verdict, and NEVER drops / rewrites / alters any verified number. NOT
492	# wired into any drop path. Gated by PG_PRIMACY_FRAME_ANNOTATE (default ON); OFF ->
493	# inert (byte-identical). Pure, stdlib-only, network-free (mirrors the
494	# effect_size_conditional_reason leg above).
495	#
496	# HIGH-PRECISION + FAIL-OPEN. Two gates keep it inert on the ambiguous majority of
497	# basket numbers (sample sizes, years, CI bounds, page numbers):
498	#   (1) SAME UNIT — both the headline and the companion must be PERCENT figures
499	#       ("46%", "46 percent"); a bare digit carries no "%" so it can never be a
500	#       companion, and a claim with no headline percent is inert immediately.
501	#   (2) SAME CONTEXT — the headline's local measure context and the companion's
502	#       local measure context must share a content-word stem (jobs / tasks /
503	#       exposed), so two unrelated percentages (e.g. "46% of tasks" vs "95% CI")
504	#       never pair.
505	# PLUS a MATERIAL magnitude gap (absolute AND ratio), so rounding neighbours
506	# ("1.8%" vs "1.9%") never fire. A percent whose context is a confidence-interval /
507	# significance level is skipped outright.
508	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
509	def primacy_frame_annotate_enabled() -> bool:
510	    """Whether the one-sidedness primacy annotate leg is active (default ON)."""
511	    return os.getenv("PG_PRIMACY_FRAME_ANNOTATE", "1").strip().lower() in _TRUE_TOKENS
512	
513	
514	# A PERCENT figure: a numeric token (the SAME ``\d+(?:\.\d+)?`` shape as
515	# ``_NUMERIC_TOKEN_RE``) immediately carrying a percent unit. Group 1 is the number
516	# string, so a headline/companion value is compared and reported in the same format
517	# ``_NUMERIC_TOKEN_RE`` yields (the omission check against the claim's numeric tokens
518	# is therefore exact).
519	_PRIMACY_PERCENT_RE = re.compile(
520	    r"(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*(?:%|percent(?:age)?\b|per\s*cent\b)",
521	    re.IGNORECASE,
522	)
523	# A percent sitting in a confidence-interval / significance context is a precision /
524	# uncertainty figure, NOT a headline measure — skip it (never a companion).
525	_PRIMACY_CI_CONTEXT_RE = re.compile(
526	    r"\bC\.?I\.?\b|confidence\s+interval|\bp\s*[<=>]|significan",
527	    re.IGNORECASE,
528	)
529	# Function words dropped when comparing the MEASURE CONTEXT of two percents, so the
530	# same-kind test keys on salient content words (jobs, tasks, exposed), not on glue.
531	_PRIMACY_STOPWORDS = frozenset({
532	    "the", "and", "for", "with", "from", "that", "this", "these", "those",
533	    "was", "were", "are", "has", "have", "had", "its", "their", "they", "them",
534	    "just", "over", "under", "about", "than", "then", "which", "could", "would",
535	    "may", "might", "into", "onto", "per", "cent", "percent", "percentage",
536	    "points", "point", "roughly", "around", "approximately", "some", "more",
537	    "most", "such", "when", "while", "also", "only", "but", "not",
538	})
539	# Alphabetic content word (>=4 letters) inside a percent's local context window.
540	_PRIMACY_WORD_RE = re.compile(r"[A-Za-z]{4,}")
541	# Light stem length + the local context window (chars each side of the percent).
542	_PRIMACY_STEM_LEN = 5
543	_PRIMACY_CONTEXT_WINDOW = 48
544	# A companion fires only on a MATERIAL magnitude gap: both an absolute
545	# percentage-point gap AND a ratio, so rounding neighbours never trip it.
546	_PRIMACY_MIN_ABS_GAP_PCT = 5.0
547	_PRIMACY_MIN_RATIO = 1.5
548	
549	
550	def _primacy_percents(text: str) -> list[tuple[str, float, frozenset]]:
551	    """Percent figures in `text` as (number_str, value, context-stems).
552	
553	    Skips a percent whose local window is a confidence-interval / significance
554	    context (a precision figure, not a headline measure). Context-stems are the
555	    5-char prefixes of content words (>=4 letters, non-stopword) within
556	    ``_PRIMACY_CONTEXT_WINDOW`` chars of the percent, CLAMPED to the percent's own
557	    sentence — so a measure noun in a DIFFERENT sentence never leaks into this
558	    percent's context (mirrors the per-sentence scoping of the effect-size leg via
559	    ``_SPAN_SENTENCE_SPLIT_RE``). This is the 'same measure kind' signal.
560	    """
561	    out: list[tuple[str, float, frozenset]] = []
562	    for sentence in _SPAN_SENTENCE_SPLIT_RE.split(text or ""):
563	        for m in _PRIMACY_PERCENT_RE.finditer(sentence):
564	            lo = max(0, m.start() - _PRIMACY_CONTEXT_WINDOW)
565	            hi = min(len(sentence), m.end() + _PRIMACY_CONTEXT_WINDOW)
566	            window = sentence[lo:hi]
567	            if _PRIMACY_CI_CONTEXT_RE.search(window):
568	                continue
569	            try:
570	                val = float(m.group(1))
571	            except ValueError:
572	                continue
573	            stems = frozenset(
574	                w.lower()[:_PRIMACY_STEM_LEN]
575	                for w in _PRIMACY_WORD_RE.findall(window)
576	                if w.lower() not in _PRIMACY_STOPWORDS
577	            )
578	            out.append((m.group(1), val, stems))
579	    return out
580	
581	
582	def primacy_frame_reason(claim: str, basket_span_text: str) -> str | None:
583	    """Return an ANNOTATE reason if the claim headlines one percent figure while the
584	    cited basket also holds a materially-different SAME-KIND companion percent the
585	    claim omits (a one-sidedness / primacy frame).
586	
587	    Fires only when ALL hold:
588	      - the leg is enabled (PG_PRIMACY_FRAME_ANNOTATE, default ON),
589	      - the claim asserts at least one PERCENT headline figure,
590	      - the basket holds a PERCENT companion whose value the claim OMITS,
591	      - that companion shares a measure-context stem with a claim headline (same
592	        unit AND same context — e.g. both "% of jobs/tasks exposed"), and
593	      - the headline<->companion magnitudes differ MATERIALLY (absolute AND ratio).
594	    Otherwise returns None (inert — bare digits: sample sizes, years, CI bounds,
595	    page numbers carry no "%", so they can never fire). ADVISORY-only: the caller
596	    APPENDS a soft-warning; this NEVER drops, widens a span, changes a verdict, or
597	    alters any verified number (§-1.3).
598	    """
599	    if not primacy_frame_annotate_enabled():
600	        return None
601	    if not claim or not basket_span_text:
602	        return None
603	    claim_bare = _CLAIM_CITATION_STRIP_RE.sub("", claim)
604	    claim_pcts = _primacy_percents(claim_bare)
605	    if not claim_pcts:
606	        return None  # no headline percent — nothing to be one-sided about
607	    basket_pcts = _primacy_percents(basket_span_text)
608	    if not basket_pcts:
609	        return None  # basket carries no same-unit companion (bare digits) — inert
610	    claim_numbers = set(_NUMERIC_TOKEN_RE.findall(claim_bare))
611	    for c_str, c_val, c_stems in basket_pcts:
612	        if c_str in claim_numbers:
613	            continue  # the claim already presents this figure — not omitted
614	        for h_str, h_val, h_stems in claim_pcts:
615	            if not (c_stems & h_stems):
616	                continue  # different measure kind (no shared context stem)
617	            gap = abs(c_val - h_val)
618	            hi_val, lo_val = max(c_val, h_val), min(c_val, h_val)
619	            if gap < _PRIMACY_MIN_ABS_GAP_PCT:
620	                continue  # not a material absolute gap (rounding neighbour)
621	            if lo_val <= 0.0 or (hi_val / lo_val) < _PRIMACY_MIN_RATIO:
622	                continue  # not a material ratio
623	            return (
624	                "primacy_frame_companion_omitted:headline="
625	                + h_str + "%:companion=" + c_str + "%"
626	            )
627	    return None
628	
629	
630	def temporal_scope_reason(claim: str, span_text: str) -> str | None:
631	    """Return a drop reason if the claim's time-horizon is not in the cited span.
632	
633	    Fires only when BOTH the claim AND the span name an explicit horizon and the
634	    claim's named horizon (duration or year-range) is absent from the span's
635	    horizons. If the span names no horizon, or the claim names none, returns None
636	    (inert — the numeric/content legs already govern those cases).
637	    """
638	    if not claim or not span_text:
639	        return None
640	
641	    claim_durs = _durations_in(claim)
642	    claim_ranges = _year_ranges_in(claim)
643	    if not claim_durs and not claim_ranges:
644	        return None  # claim makes no temporal-scope assertion
645	
646	    span_durs = _durations_in(span_text)
647	    span_ranges = _year_ranges_in(span_text)
648	    if not span_durs and not span_ranges:
649	        return None  # span carries no horizon to compare against — inert
650	
651	    # Fire ONLY on a genuine WIDENING: a claim horizon whose MAGNITUDE exceeds
652	    # the span's LONGEST horizon by more than the tolerance. Comparing raw
653	    # (count, unit) tuples here over-drops legitimately-scoped claims — "12
654	    # months" vs "one year", "52 weeks" vs "one year", or a NARROWER claim
655	    # horizon are all supported by the cited span and must pass
656	    # (I-deepfix-001 Codex P1). Equivalence/narrowing -> no drop; only a horizon
657	    # longer than the evidence supports is an overstatement.
658	    span_max_days = _max_horizon_days(span_durs, span_ranges)
659	    widen_ceiling = span_max_days * (1.0 + _TEMPORAL_WIDEN_REL_TOLERANCE)
660	    missing_durs = {
661	        (n, u) for (n, u) in claim_durs if _duration_days(n, u) > widen_ceiling
662	    }
663	    missing_ranges = {
664	        (a, b) for (a, b) in claim_ranges if _range_days(a, b) > widen_ceiling
665	    }
666	    if not missing_durs and not missing_ranges:
667	        return None
668	
669	    parts: list[str] = []
670	    if missing_durs:
671	        parts.append(
672	            "durations=" + ",".join(f"{n}{u}" for n, u in sorted(missing_durs))
673	        )
674	    if missing_ranges:
675	        parts.append(
676	            "ranges=" + ",".join(f"{a}-{b}" for a, b in sorted(missing_ranges))
677	        )
678	    return "temporal_scope_mismatch:" + ":".join(parts)
679

681	# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
682	# I-deepfix-001 group w4-SL — four ADDITIVE, faithfulness-TIGHTENING drop legs
683	# (S5 / L1 / L2 / L3). Every one is pure, stdlib-only, network-free, env-gated
684	# DEFAULT-ON, and STRICTLY ADDITIVE: it can only APPEND a drop reason. None ever
685	# clears an existing failure, relaxes an existing check, widens a span, or changes
686	# a verdict — so setting its kill-switch to a falsy value reverts the leg
687	# BYTE-IDENTICAL. They are wired into ``verify_sentence_provenance`` (the BeatBoth
688	# composer / abstractive-writer strict-verify path) against the SAME cited-span
689	# aggregate the numeric/content legs use. DNA (§-1.3): the faithfulness engine is
690	# the only hard gate; these TIGHTEN it, they never relax it. UNDER-DROP IS SAFE;
691	# OVER-DROP hurts only breadth (the composer's verbatim K-span fallback still
692	# ships the faithful, qualifier-carrying source text). Each leg is HIGH-PRECISION
693	# + FAIL-OPEN — inert on the ambiguous majority, firing only on a clear defect.
694	# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
695	
696	
697	# ── S5 — span-faithful qualifier retention on HEADLINE numerics ───────────────
698	#
699	# A headline figure ("just over 46% of jobs are exposed", "up to 46 million jobs
700	# could be automated") whose cited span binds the number to a governing
701	# CONDITIONAL / THRESHOLD antecedent ("when accounting for current and likely
702	# future software developments", "if adoption accelerates", "up to") but whose
703	# CLAIM drops that antecedent asserts UNCONDITIONALLY what the source stated only
704	# under the condition. This is the render-layer ``effect_size_conditional_reason``
705	# ANNOTATE leg promoted to a strict-verify DROP: the composer then falls back to
706	# the member's verbatim K-span, which retains the condition by construction. It is
707	# distinct from the P5 epistemic-qualifier gate (which keys on HEDGE markers and
708	# EXCLUDES bare integers): S5 keys on CONDITIONAL/THRESHOLD clauses and covers
709	# unit-bearing INTEGER headline numerics the P5 substantive-numeral set skips.
710	def numeric_qualifier_retention_enabled() -> bool:
711	    """Whether the S5 headline-numeric conditional-retention DROP leg is active
712	    (default ON). Kill-switch PG_STRICT_VERIFY_NUMERIC_QUALIFIER_RETENTION=0
713	    reverts byte-identical."""
714	    return (
715	        os.getenv("PG_STRICT_VERIFY_NUMERIC_QUALIFIER_RETENTION", "1").strip().lower()
716	        in _TRUE_TOKENS
717	    )
718	
719	
720	# S5 CLAIM-CARRIED antecedent set (I-deepfix-001 Codex P1, iter 1). The render-layer
721	# ``effect_size_conditional_reason`` treats a bare epistemic modal ("could"/"may"/
722	# "might"/"would") as a carried antecedent — appropriate there because that leg only
723	# APPENDS a soft caveat, never drops. But for the S5 DROP leg a bare modal is NOT a
724	# threshold ("up to") and NOT a conditional ("if ..."/"when accounting for ..."): a
725	# claim that keeps only "could" while dropping the span's governing "up to N ... if
726	# ..." antecedent asserts the number MORE strongly than the source stated it. So
727	# "46 million jobs could be automated", derived from "Up to 46 million jobs could be
728	# automated if firms accelerate adoption", must DROP — the "up to" ceiling and the
729	# "if" precondition both vanished and a modal does not carry either. This set is the
730	# shared ``_CLAIM_ANTECEDENT_CARRIED_RE`` MINUS the bare-modal alternation, so S5
731	# fires on the modal-only case without changing the annotate leg's behaviour.
732	_S5_CLAIM_ANTECEDENT_CARRIED_RE = re.compile(
733	    r"(?:"
734	    r"account(?:ing|s|ed)?\s+for"
735	    r"|\bup\s+to\b|\bas\s+(?:much|many)\s+as\b|\bover\s+half\b"
736	    r"|\bif\b|\bassum(?:e|es|ed|ing)\b|\bprovided\b"
737	    r"|\bscenario\b|\bhypothetical(?:ly)?\b"
738	    r"|\bwhen\b"
739	    r"|future\s+(?:software|developments|technolog)"
740	    r")",
741	    re.IGNORECASE,
742	)
743	
744	
745	def numeric_qualifier_retention_reason(claim: str, span_text: str) -> str | None:
746	    """Return a DROP reason when the claim re-lifts a span number while dropping
747	    the conditional/threshold antecedent that governs it in the cited span.
748	
749	    Fires only when ALL hold: the leg is enabled; the claim carries NO governing
750	    conditional/threshold of its own (a bare assertion); the cited span has a
751	    sentence whose governing conditional/threshold token PRECEDES a number; and the
752	    claim reproduces that governed number. Otherwise returns None (inert — no false
753	    drop). DROP-severity (unlike ``effect_size_conditional_reason`` which annotates)
754	    so the composer falls back to the qualifier-carrying verbatim K-span (§-1.3
755	    faithfulness tightening; under-drop is the safe direction)."""
756	    if not numeric_qualifier_retention_enabled():
757	        return None
758	    if not claim or not span_text:
759	        return None
760	    claim_bare = _CLAIM_CITATION_STRIP_RE.sub("", claim)
761	    claim_numbers = set(_NUMERIC_TOKEN_RE.findall(claim_bare))
762	    if not claim_numbers:
763	        return None  # no figure re-lifted — nothing to over-assert
764	    if _S5_CLAIM_ANTECEDENT_CARRIED_RE.search(claim_bare):
765	        return None  # the claim carries a real threshold/conditional antecedent — faithful
766	    for sentence in _SPAN_SENTENCE_SPLIT_RE.split(span_text):
767	        cond = _SPAN_EFFECT_CONDITION_RE.search(sentence)
768	        if not cond:
769	            continue
770	        governed = set(_NUMERIC_TOKEN_RE.findall(sentence[cond.start():]))
771	        shared = claim_numbers & governed
772	        if shared:
773	            return "headline_numeric_qualifier_dropped:num=" + ",".join(sorted(shared))
774	    return None
775

777	# ── L2 — numeric-fidelity span re-check: VALUE AND ROLE (currency + multiplier) ─
778	#
779	# Extends the percent-role match (``PG_PROVENANCE_PERCENT_ROLE_MATCH``) to two more
780	# measure ROLES so a printed number must appear in its cited span in the SAME role,
781	# not merely as a bare digit (a page number / a year / a reference index):
782	#   * CURRENCY   — "$14 billion" must be grounded by a currency figure of the same
783	#                  value (and, when BOTH sides name a scale, the same scale — so
784	#                  "$14 billion" is NOT grounded by "$14 million").
785	#   * MULTIPLIER — "14-fold" / "14× higher" must be grounded by a same-value
786	#                  multiplier, not by a bare "14" elsewhere in the span.
787	# FAIL-OPEN: a claim value with NO scale is satisfied by ANY same-value currency in
788	# the span (a scale conflict fires ONLY when both sides carry a differing, non-empty
789	# scale) — so an abbreviation the extractor does not recognize can never false-drop.
790	_L2_SCALE_WORD = r"(?:thousand|million|billion|trillion)"
791	_L2_CUR_SYMBOL = r"[$€£¥₹]"
792	_L2_CUR_CODE = r"(?:USD|EUR|GBP|JPY|CAD|AUD|CHF|CNY|INR)"
793	_L2_CUR_WORD = r"(?:dollars?|euros?|pounds?|yen|francs?|yuan|rupees?)"
794	# symbol / ISO-code BEFORE the number, optional scale word after ("$14", "$14 billion",
795	# "USD 14 million").
796	_L2_CURRENCY_PRE_RE = re.compile(
797	    r"(?:" + _L2_CUR_SYMBOL + r"|\b" + _L2_CUR_CODE + r"\b\s?)"
798	    r"(\d+(?:[.,]\d+)?)\s*(" + _L2_SCALE_WORD + r")?",
799	    re.IGNORECASE,
800	)
801	# number then optional scale then currency WORD ("14 billion dollars", "14 euros").
802	_L2_CURRENCY_POST_RE = re.compile(
803	    r"(\d+(?:[.,]\d+)?)\s*(" + _L2_SCALE_WORD + r")?\s*" + _L2_CUR_WORD + r"\b",
804	    re.IGNORECASE,
805	)
806	# A MULTIPLIER: N-fold / N× / N x / "N times <comparative>" (bare "N times" without a
807	# comparative is frequency, not a magnitude — deliberately excluded for precision).
808	_L2_MULTIPLIER_RE = re.compile(
809	    r"(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*(?:"
810	    r"-?\s*fold\b"
811	    r"|×"
812	    r"|x(?=[\s.,;:)]|$)"
813	    r"|\s*times\s+(?:higher|greater|lower|smaller|larger|more|less|faster|slower|"
814	    r"as\s+(?:many|much|likely))"
815	    r")",
816	    re.IGNORECASE,
817	)
818	
819	
820	def _l2_currency_entries(text: str) -> "set[tuple[str, str]]":
821	    """Currency figures in `text` as a set of (value, scale) — value comma-stripped,
822	    scale lower-cased or "" when none. Reads both symbol/code-before and word-after
823	    forms so "$14 billion" and "14 billion dollars" canonicalize identically."""
824	    out: set[tuple[str, str]] = set()
825	    for rx in (_L2_CURRENCY_PRE_RE, _L2_CURRENCY_POST_RE):
826	        for m in rx.finditer(text or ""):
827	            val = (m.group(1) or "").replace(",", "")
828	            scale = (m.group(2) or "").lower()
829	            if val:
830	                out.add((val, scale))
831	    return out
832	
833	
834	def _l2_multiplier_values(text: str) -> "set[str]":
835	    """Multiplier magnitudes in `text` ("14-fold", "14×", "14 times higher") as the
836	    set of value strings."""
837	    return {m.group(1) for m in _L2_MULTIPLIER_RE.finditer(text or "")}
838	
839	
840	def numeric_role_match_enabled() -> bool:
841	    """Whether the L2 currency/multiplier VALUE-AND-ROLE DROP leg is active (default
842	    ON). Kill-switch PG_PROVENANCE_NUMERIC_ROLE_MATCH=0 reverts byte-identical."""
843	    return (
844	        os.getenv("PG_PROVENANCE_NUMERIC_ROLE_MATCH", "1").strip().lower()
845	        in _TRUE_TOKENS
846	    )
847	
848	
849	def numeric_role_match_reason(claim: str, span_text: str) -> str | None:
850	    """Return a DROP reason when the claim prints a CURRENCY or MULTIPLIER figure
851	    whose value+role is not grounded by the SAME role in the cited span.
852	
853	    High-precision + fail-open. Currency: a claim value missing from the span's
854	    currency figures drops; a claim value present but with a CONFLICTING non-empty
855	    scale (billion vs million) drops; a claim value with no scale, or a span figure
856	    with no recognized scale, never conflicts. Multiplier: a claim multiplier value
857	    absent from the span's multipliers drops. A claim carrying no currency/multiplier
858	    figure is inert (the percent-role + numeric legs already govern bare digits)."""
859	    if not numeric_role_match_enabled():
860	        return None
861	    if not claim or not span_text:
862	        return None
863	    claim_bare = _CLAIM_CITATION_STRIP_RE.sub("", claim)
864	    parts: list[str] = []
865	
866	    claim_cur = _l2_currency_entries(claim_bare)
867	    if claim_cur:
868	        span_cur = _l2_currency_entries(span_text)
869	        span_scales_by_val: dict[str, set[str]] = {}
870	        for val, scale in span_cur:
871	            span_scales_by_val.setdefault(val, set()).add(scale)
872	        missing_cur: set[str] = set()
873	        for val, scale in claim_cur:
874	            if val not in span_scales_by_val:
875	                missing_cur.add(f"{val}:{scale or '-'}")  # value never appears AS currency
876	            elif scale and all(
877	                s and s != scale for s in span_scales_by_val[val]
878	            ):
879	                missing_cur.add(f"{val}:{scale}")  # value present but every span scale conflicts
880	        if missing_cur:
881	            parts.append("currency=" + ",".join(sorted(missing_cur)))
882	
883	    claim_mul = _l2_multiplier_values(claim_bare)
884	    if claim_mul:
885	        missing_mul = claim_mul - _l2_multiplier_values(span_text)
886	        if missing_mul:
887	            parts.append("multiplier=" + ",".join(sorted(missing_mul)))
888	
889	    if parts:
890	        return "numeric_role_mismatch:" + ":".join(parts)
891	    return None
892	
893	
894	# ── L1 — judge the clinical qualifier as a UNIT (number + population/indication) ─
895	#
896	# A span-faithful number can be bound to the WRONG population / indication and still
897	# pass span-grounding — the exact failure that hurts patients. This leg judges the
898	# number together with the governing clinical POPULATION/INDICATION antecedent the
899	# span binds to it (the effect-size-conditional pattern extended to clinical
900	# qualifiers): if the span binds a shared number to a population token within a
901	# proximity window and the CLAIM carries neither that population nor a synonym of it
902	# (dropped OR conflicting population), DROP. High-precision curated population
903	# lexicon; population tokens are stem-matched so plural/singular never false-drops.
904	_L1_POPULATION_RE = re.compile(
905	    r"\b(?:"
906	    r"immunocompromised|immunosuppressed|immunodeficient"
907	    r"|pregnan(?:t|cy)|breastfeed\w*|lactating|neonat\w*|infants?|newborns?"
908	    r"|children|child|paediatric|pediatric|adolescents?|elderly|geriatric|adults?"
909	    r"|renal(?:\s+impair\w+)?|hepatic(?:\s+impair\w+)?|dialysis"
910	    r"|diabetic|diabetes|hypertensive|cirrho\w+|immunonaive"
911	    r"|treatment-naive|treatment-experienced|opioid-naive"
912	    r")\b",
913	    re.IGNORECASE,
914	)
915	
916	
917	def _l1_pop_stem(token: str) -> str:
918	    """A short case-folded stem for a population token so plural/singular and light
919	    inflection ("adults"/"adult", "infants"/"infant") match; keeps precision by
920	    stripping only a trailing 's'."""
921	    t = re.sub(r"s$", "", token.lower())
922	    return t[:6]
923	
924	
925	def _l1_pop_stems(text: str) -> "set[str]":
926	    return {_l1_pop_stem(m.group(0)) for m in _L1_POPULATION_RE.finditer(text or "")}
927	
928	
929	def _l1_span_populations_near(sentence: str, number: str, window: int) -> "set[str]":
930	    """Population stems within `window` whitespace tokens of an occurrence of
931	    `number` (whole-number match) inside `sentence`."""
932	    toks = sentence.split()
933	    pops: set[str] = set()
934	    for i, tok in enumerate(toks):
935	        if number not in _NUMERIC_TOKEN_RE.findall(tok):
936	            continue
937	        lo = max(0, i - window)
938	        hi = min(len(toks), i + window + 1)
939	        pops |= _l1_pop_stems(" ".join(toks[lo:hi]))
940	    return pops
941	
942	
943	def clinical_qualifier_unit_enabled() -> bool:
944	    """Whether the L1 clinical population/indication-unit DROP leg is active
945	    (default ON). Kill-switch PG_STRICT_VERIFY_CLINICAL_QUALIFIER_UNIT=0 reverts
946	    byte-identical."""
947	    return (
948	        os.getenv("PG_STRICT_VERIFY_CLINICAL_QUALIFIER_UNIT", "1").strip().lower()
949	        in _TRUE_TOKENS
950	    )
951	
952	
953	# Proximity window (whitespace tokens) binding a population token to a number.
954	_L1_PROXIMITY_TOKENS = 12
955	
956	
957	def clinical_qualifier_unit_reason(claim: str, span_text: str) -> str | None:
958	    """Return a DROP reason when the span binds a shared number to a clinical
959	    population/indication the claim fails to carry.
960	
961	    Fires only when a span SENTENCE co-locates a shared number and a curated
962	    population token within the proximity window AND none of that sentence's
963	    bound-population stems appears in the claim. Otherwise inert (no population
964	    binding in the span, or the claim carries the population -> the qualifier
965	    travelled with the number). DROP so the composer falls back to the K-span that
966	    keeps the number bound to its population (§-1.3 clinical-safety tightening —
967	    over-crediting a mis-populated number is lethal; under-drop is safe)."""
968	    if not clinical_qualifier_unit_enabled():
969	        return None
970	    if not claim or not span_text:
971	        return None
972	    claim_bare = _CLAIM_CITATION_STRIP_RE.sub("", claim)
973	    claim_numbers = set(_NUMERIC_TOKEN_RE.findall(claim_bare))
974	    if not claim_numbers:
975	        return None  # no number to mis-bind
976	    claim_pops = _l1_pop_stems(claim_bare)
977	    for sentence in _SPAN_SENTENCE_SPLIT_RE.split(span_text):
978	        shared = claim_numbers & set(_NUMERIC_TOKEN_RE.findall(sentence))
979	        for number in sorted(shared):
980	            span_pops = _l1_span_populations_near(
981	                sentence, number, _L1_PROXIMITY_TOKENS
982	            )
983	            if span_pops and not (span_pops & claim_pops):
984	                return (
985	                    "clinical_qualifier_unit_dropped:num=" + number
986	                    + ":span_pop=" + ",".join(sorted(span_pops))
987	                )
988	    return None
989	
990	
991	# ── L3 — negation / contraindication semantic polarity guard ──────────────────
992	#
993	# "not contraindicated" must never render from a "contraindicated" span (and
994	# vice-versa). For a curated set of CLINICAL RELATION stems, if the claim's polarity
995	# toward the stem (negated vs asserted) DISAGREES with the cited span's polarity for
996	# the SAME stem, DROP. Deterministic pre-stem negation window + expanded negative
997	# contractions; a mention is treated as negated if ANY occurrence is negated
998	# (conservative — a report that inverts a contraindication is §-1.1-lethal).
999	_L3_RELATION_STEMS: tuple[str, ...] = (
1000	    "contraindicat",
1001	    "recommend",
1002	    "indicated",
1003	    "approv",
1004	    "efficac",
1005	    "effective",
1006	    "superior",
1007	    "toler",         # tolerated / tolerability
1008	    "benefici",
1009	    "associat",
1010	    "respons",       # responsive / response
1011	    "eligib",
1012	    "safe",
1013	)
1014	# Negative contractions -> "X not" so the pre-stem negator fires on contraction spelling.
1015	_L3_NEG_CONTRACTIONS = {
1016	    "aren't": "are not", "isn't": "is not", "wasn't": "was not", "weren't": "were not",
1017	    "haven't": "have not", "hasn't": "has not", "hadn't": "had not",
1018	    "don't": "do not", "doesn't": "does not", "didn't": "did not",
1019	    "won't": "will not", "wouldn't": "would not", "can't": "can not", "couldn't": "could not",
1020	    "shouldn't": "should not", "mustn't": "must not", "needn't": "need not",
1021	    "cannot": "can not",
1022	}
1023	# A negation cue that, appearing shortly BEFORE the stem (<=30 interposed chars),
1024	# inverts it: "not contraindicated", "no known contraindication", "never
1025	# recommended", "fails to be effective", "not associated with".
1026	_L3_NEG_BEFORE_RE = re.compile(
1027	    r"\b(?:no|not|never|without|none|neither|nor|lacks?|lacking|absent|"
1028	    r"absence\s+of|no\s+evidence\s+of|fails?\s+to|failed\s+to|"
1029	    r"free\s+of|devoid\s+of|unlikely\s+to\s+be|ruled?\s+out|rules?\s+out)\b"
1030	    r"[\w\s,'()/-]{0,30}?$",
1031	    re.IGNORECASE,
1032	)
1033	
1034	
1035	def _l3_expand(text: str) -> str:
1036	    text = text.replace("’", "'").lower()
1037	    for contraction, expanded in _L3_NEG_CONTRACTIONS.items():
1038	        text = text.replace(contraction, expanded)
1039	    return text
1040	
1041	
1042	def _l3_relation_polarity(text: str, stem: str) -> "bool | None":
1043	    """None if `stem` is absent; True if any occurrence is negated by a pre-stem
1044	    cue; False if the stem is present and no occurrence is negated."""
1045	    negated = False
1046	    found = False
1047	    for m in re.finditer(stem, text):
1048	        found = True
1049	        pre = text[max(0, m.start() - 40):m.start()]
1050	        if _L3_NEG_BEFORE_RE.search(pre):
1051	            negated = True
1052	            break
1053	    if not found:
1054	        return None
1055	    return negated
1056	
1057	
1058	def clinical_polarity_guard_enabled() -> bool:
1059	    """Whether the L3 clinical negation/contraindication polarity DROP leg is active
1060	    (default ON). Kill-switch PG_STRICT_VERIFY_CLINICAL_POLARITY=0 reverts
1061	    byte-identical."""
1062	    return (
1063	        os.getenv("PG_STRICT_VERIFY_CLINICAL_POLARITY", "1").strip().lower()
1064	        in _TRUE_TOKENS
1065	    )
1066	
1067	
1068	def clinical_polarity_reason(claim: str, span_text: str) -> str | None:
1069	    """Return a DROP reason when a clinical relation stem shared by the claim and its
1070	    cited span carries OPPOSITE polarity (one negated, one asserted). Inert when no
1071	    shared stem, or both polarities agree. DROP — a negated clinical relation
1072	    rendered from a non-negated span (or vice-versa) is a lethal inversion (§-1.3)."""
1073	    if not clinical_polarity_guard_enabled():
1074	        return None
1075	    if not claim or not span_text:
1076	        return None
1077	    claim_norm = _l3_expand(_CLAIM_CITATION_STRIP_RE.sub("", claim))
1078	    span_norm = _l3_expand(span_text)
1079	    for stem in _L3_RELATION_STEMS:
1080	        pc = _l3_relation_polarity(claim_norm, stem)
1081	        if pc is None:
1082	            continue
1083	        ps = _l3_relation_polarity(span_norm, stem)
1084	        if ps is None:
1085	            continue
1086	        if pc != ps:
1087	            return (
1088	                "clinical_polarity_mismatch:stem=" + stem
1089	                + ":claim=" + ("neg" if pc else "pos")
1090	                + ":span=" + ("neg" if ps else "pos")
1091	            )
1092	    return None
1093

1	"""Deterministic verified-number COMPARISON TABLE renderer (I-deepfix-001 Wave-2c, #1344).
2	
3	Some research prompts (and the DRB-II "Presentation" rubric explicitly) reward presenting a
4	like-with-like numeric comparison in TABLE form — the rubric example is "presented the change
5	in gold prices in table form": the same measure and unit, with values across entities or
6	time-points. The multi-section generator emits span-verified narrative prose but does not lay
7	out such a comparison table, so the presentation dimension loses a directly-scored point.
8	
9	This module renders that table WITHOUT any LLM / network / IO, purely from ALREADY-VERIFIED
10	numeric claims. It is presentation-only and therefore faithfulness-NEUTRAL (CLAUDE.md §-1.3: a
11	table is a presentation of already-verified findings, not a new claim). The contract:
12	
13	* Consumes ONLY already-verified numeric claims supplied by the caller. It performs NO
14	  verification and NEVER re-verifies — it does not touch strict_verify / NLI / 4-role D8 /
15	  provenance / span-grounding.
16	* Numbers are copied VERBATIM from the verified claim (never recomputed, never rounded, never
17	  reformatted). No arithmetic is performed on any value.
18	* Each value keeps its citation marker (``[N]`` / ``[#ev:...]``) verbatim, rendered in the
19	  Citation column of its own row.
20	* A table is emitted ONLY when >= 2 comparable claims share a (measure, unit) — otherwise the
21	  module returns empty (no single-row filler, no invented comparison).
22	
23	Gated behind the default-OFF kill-switch ``PG_PRESENTATION_TABLES`` (LAW VI). OFF => the public
24	entry returns a no-op (``changed=False``, ``text=""``) so a future caller that only inserts
25	``result.text`` when ``result.changed`` is byte-identical to today. This build is MODULE ONLY:
26	no caller is wired to it yet (that is a separate Batch-2 ``2c-wiring`` step).
27	"""
28	
29	from __future__ import annotations
30	
31	import os
32	import re
33	from dataclasses import dataclass
34	from typing import Any, Iterable
35	
36	# --- canary / markers -------------------------------------------------------
37	CANARY_TAG = "[presentation_tables]"
38	# Distinctive idempotency marker embedded once per rendered table (HTML comment, invisible in a
39	# markdown viewer). A resume / re-finalize that re-reads a report already carrying this marker is
40	# a no-op at the public entry.
41	TABLE_MARKER = "<!-- polaris:presentation_table -->"
42	
43	# --- env kill-switch (default OFF; LAW VI) ----------------------------------
44	_ENABLE_FLAG = "PG_PRESENTATION_TABLES"
45	_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"0", "false", "off", "no", ""})
46	
47	# --- comparability gate -----------------------------------------------------
48	# A table is emitted only when at least this many comparable claims share a (measure, unit).
49	_MIN_COMPARABLE = 2
50	
51	# --- disclosed-gap marker ---------------------------------------------------
52	GAP_CELL = "—"
53	
54	# --- rendering --------------------------------------------------------------
55	_ENTITY_HEADER_DEFAULT = "Entity"
56	_WS_RE = re.compile(r"\s+")
57	
58	
59	@dataclass(frozen=True)
60	class VerifiedNumericClaim:
61	    """One ALREADY-VERIFIED numeric claim for a facet.
62	
63	    ``value`` is a VERBATIM string (or the caller may pass a list which is joined verbatim by
64	    :func:`_value_str`); it is never parsed to a number, so no rounding/reformatting can occur.
65	    ``citation`` is the verbatim citation marker (e.g. ``[3]`` or a ``[#ev:...]`` token).
66	    """
67	
68	    entity: str
69	    measure: str
70	    value: str
71	    unit: str = ""
72	    time_window: str = ""
73	    citation: str = ""
74	
75	
76	@dataclass
77	class PresentationTablesResult:
78	    text: str
79	    changed: bool
80	    tables: int = 0
81	    rows: int = 0
82	    canary: str = ""
83	
84	
85	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
86	# Enablement
87	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
88	def presentation_tables_enabled() -> bool:
89	    """LAW VI kill-switch (default OFF). OFF => the public entry is a no-op."""
90	    return os.environ.get(_ENABLE_FLAG, "0").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES
91	
92	
93	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
94	# Input coercion
95	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
96	def _get(obj: Any, name: str, default: Any = "") -> Any:
97	    if isinstance(obj, dict):
98	        return obj.get(name, default)
99	    return getattr(obj, name, default)
100	
101	
102	def _first_nonempty(obj: Any, names: tuple[str, ...]) -> str:
103	    """First non-empty stripped string among ``names`` on ``obj`` (dict or attr). PURE."""
104	    for name in names:
105	        val = _get(obj, name, "")
106	        if val is None:
107	            continue
108	        text = str(val).strip()
109	        if text:
110	            return text
111	    return ""
112	
113	
114	def _value_str(value: Any) -> str:
115	    """VERBATIM rendering of a value. A str/scalar is returned stripped and OTHERWISE unchanged
116	    (no float parse => no rounding/reformatting); a list/tuple is joined with "; " keeping each
117	    token verbatim. Empty tokens are dropped. PURE."""
118	    if isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
119	        return "; ".join(str(v).strip() for v in value if str(v).strip())
120	    if value is None:
121	        return ""
122	    return str(value).strip()
123	
124	
125	def _coerce_claim(obj: Any) -> VerifiedNumericClaim | None:
126	    """Duck-type ``obj`` (a ``VerifiedNumericClaim``, a dict, or an object) into a
127	    ``VerifiedNumericClaim``. Field fallbacks match the pipeline's ``ExtractedNumericClaim``
128	    (subject/predicate/value/unit) so a future wiring step can feed real claims unchanged. A
129	    claim missing entity, measure, or value is dropped (no partial-field row invented). PURE."""
130	    if obj is None:
131	        return None
132	    entity = _first_nonempty(obj, ("entity", "subject"))
133	    measure = _first_nonempty(obj, ("measure", "predicate", "endpoint_phrase"))
134	    value = _value_str(_get(obj, "value", ""))
135	    if not (entity and measure and value):
136	        return None
137	    unit = _first_nonempty(obj, ("unit",))
138	    time_window = _first_nonempty(obj, ("time_window", "window"))
139	    citation = _first_nonempty(obj, ("citation", "cite", "marker"))
140	    return VerifiedNumericClaim(
141	        entity=entity,
142	        measure=measure,
143	        value=value,
144	        unit=unit,
145	        time_window=time_window,
146	        citation=citation,
147	    )
148	
149	
150	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
151	# Grouping (comparability)
152	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
153	def _comparability_key(claim: VerifiedNumericClaim) -> tuple[str, str]:
154	    """(measure, unit) normalized for GROUPING ONLY (lowercased, whitespace-collapsed). The
155	    rendered labels remain the verbatim surface forms. PURE."""
156	    measure = _WS_RE.sub(" ", claim.measure.strip().lower())
157	    unit = _WS_RE.sub(" ", claim.unit.strip().lower())
158	    return (measure, unit)
159	
160	
161	def group_comparable_claims(
162	    claims: Iterable[VerifiedNumericClaim],
163	) -> dict[tuple[str, str], list[VerifiedNumericClaim]]:
164	    """Group already-coerced claims by comparability key, keeping ONLY groups with
165	    ``>= _MIN_COMPARABLE`` members (no single-row filler). Returned dict is ordered
166	    deterministically by (measure, unit) key so multiple tables emit in a stable order. PURE."""
167	    buckets: dict[tuple[str, str], list[VerifiedNumericClaim]] = {}
168	    for claim in claims:
169	        buckets.setdefault(_comparability_key(claim), []).append(claim)
170	    qualifying = {k: v for k, v in buckets.items() if len(v) >= _MIN_COMPARABLE}
171	    return {k: qualifying[k] for k in sorted(qualifying)}
172	
173	
174	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
175	# Markdown rendering
176	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
177	def _escape_cell(text: str) -> str:
178	    """Newline-flatten + pipe-escape a markdown cell. PURE."""
179	    return (text or "").replace("\n", " ").replace("|", "\\|").strip()
180	
181	
182	def _entity_cell(claim: VerifiedNumericClaim) -> str:
183	    """Row identity cell: the entity, with a verbatim time-window appended when present (both
184	    strings come from the verified claim). PURE."""
185	    if claim.time_window:
186	        return f"{claim.entity} ({claim.time_window})"
187	    return claim.entity
188	
189	
190	def render_comparison_table(
191	    claims: list[VerifiedNumericClaim],
192	    *,
193	    facet_label: str = "",
194	    entity_header: str = _ENTITY_HEADER_DEFAULT,
195	) -> str:
196	    """Render one GFM comparison table for a group of comparable verified claims.
197	
198	    Rows are sorted by (entity, measure, time_window, value) so the ordering is FULLY
199	    input-order-independent — the flagship same-entity/same-measure case (one entity, several
200	    time-points) is disambiguated by time_window/value rather than falling back to input order.
201	    Every value/citation is copied VERBATIM. The measure/unit labels use the first row's surface
202	    form (all rows in a group share the normalized measure/unit). Returns "" for an empty group
203	    (documented building block; never IndexErrors). PURE."""
204	    if not claims:
205	        return ""
206	    rows = sorted(
207	        claims,
208	        key=lambda c: (
209	            c.entity.strip().lower(),
210	            c.measure.strip().lower(),
211	            c.time_window.strip().lower(),
212	            c.value.strip().lower(),
213	        ),
214	    )
215	    measure_label = rows[0].measure.strip()
216	    headers = [entity_header, "Measure", "Value", "Unit", "Citation"]
217	
218	    title_bits = [b for b in (facet_label.strip() if facet_label else "", measure_label) if b]
219	    title = " — ".join(title_bits) if title_bits else (measure_label or "Comparison")
220	
221	    lines: list[str] = []
222	    lines.append(f"### {title}")
223	    lines.append("")
224	    lines.append(TABLE_MARKER)
225	    lines.append(
226	        "_Values are quoted verbatim from cited sources; a — marks a field the cited source "
227	        "did not provide._"
228	    )
229	    lines.append("")
230	    lines.append("| " + " | ".join(_escape_cell(h) for h in headers) + " |")
231	    lines.append("| " + " | ".join(["---"] * len(headers)) + " |")
232	    for claim in rows:
233	        cells = [
234	            _escape_cell(_entity_cell(claim)) or GAP_CELL,
235	            _escape_cell(claim.measure) or GAP_CELL,
236	            _escape_cell(claim.value) or GAP_CELL,
237	            _escape_cell(claim.unit) or GAP_CELL,
238	            _escape_cell(claim.citation) or GAP_CELL,
239	        ]
240	        lines.append("| " + " | ".join(cells) + " |")
241	    return "\n".join(lines)
242	
243	
244	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
245	# Public entry (flag-gated)
246	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
247	def render_presentation_tables(
248	    *,
249	    claims: Iterable[Any],
250	    existing_report_md: str = "",
251	    facet_label: str = "",
252	    entity_header: str = _ENTITY_HEADER_DEFAULT,
253	) -> PresentationTablesResult:
254	    """Build the verified-number comparison table(s) for a facet's already-verified numeric
255	    claims, optionally appended to ``existing_report_md``.
256	
257	    No-op (``changed=False``) when: the kill-switch is OFF, the report already carries the table
258	    marker (idempotent / resume-safe), or fewer than ``_MIN_COMPARABLE`` claims share any
259	    (measure, unit). Otherwise returns the concatenated table(s) (one per qualifying comparability
260	    group). PURE apart from reading the env kill-switch.
261	
262	    Wiring-time contract a caller MUST respect:
263	
264	    * Insert ONLY when ``result.changed`` is True; never assume ``result.text`` is the tables.
265	      The ``text`` semantics differ across the no-op paths: the OFF path returns ``text=""`` while
266	      the no-comparable / already-present paths return ``existing_report_md`` unchanged. Gating on
267	      ``changed`` keeps the OFF path byte-identical.
268	    * ``TABLE_MARKER`` idempotency is REPORT-GLOBAL: once ANY presentation table exists in the
269	      report, a later call for a DIFFERENT facet is a silent no-op (``already_present``). A
270	      multi-facet caller must therefore render ALL facets in ONE call (pass all claims together),
271	      not once per facet against the growing report."""
272	    if not presentation_tables_enabled():
273	        return PresentationTablesResult(text="", changed=False, canary="disabled")
274	    if existing_report_md and TABLE_MARKER in existing_report_md:
275	        return PresentationTablesResult(
276	            text=existing_report_md, changed=False, canary="already_present"
277	        )
278	
279	    coerced = [c for c in (_coerce_claim(x) for x in (claims or [])) if c is not None]
280	    groups = group_comparable_claims(coerced)
281	    if not groups:
282	        empty = existing_report_md if existing_report_md else ""
283	        return PresentationTablesResult(text=empty, changed=False, canary="no_comparable_claims")
284	
285	    blocks: list[str] = []
286	    total_rows = 0
287	    for grp in groups.values():
288	        blocks.append(
289	            render_comparison_table(grp, facet_label=facet_label, entity_header=entity_header)
290	        )
291	        total_rows += len(grp)
292	    tables_md = "\n\n".join(blocks)
293	
294	    if existing_report_md:
295	        new_text = existing_report_md.rstrip() + "\n\n" + tables_md + "\n"
296	    else:
297	        new_text = tables_md
298	
299	    canary = f"{CANARY_TAG} tables={len(groups)} rows={total_rows}"
300	    return PresentationTablesResult(
301	        text=new_text, changed=True, tables=len(groups), rows=total_rows, canary=canary
302	    )
303
